view.s6011.h1.title: // § 6011 REPORTABLE TRANSACTION DISCLOSURE (Form 8886)
view.s6011.hint.intro: Form 8886 reports participation in 5 categories of reportable transactions. § 6707A penalty up to $200K for listed transactions. § 6501(c)(10) extends SOL.
view.s6015.eq.legal_obligation: Legal obligation to pay (against if assumed)
view.s6015.h1.title: // § 6015 INNOCENT SPOUSE RELIEF
view.s6015.hint.intro: 3 forms: (b) Traditional Innocent Spouse, (c) Separation of Liability, (f) Equitable Relief. Relieves spouse of joint + several liability for tax on joint return attributable to other spouse. Form 8857. 2-year deadline for (b) + (c) from first IRS collection activity; (f) equitable within § 6502 CSED 10-yr period. Notice 2024-26 updated rules.
view.s6015.injured.body: Injured spouse: wants own SHARE of joint refund preserved when IRS plans to offset for spouse's separate debt (child support, student loans, back taxes). Filed simultaneously with return or up to 3 yrs after. Innocent spouse: wants relief from joint liability already assessed. Different forms, different facts, different timing.
view.s6015.label.knew: Knew or had reason to know?
view.s6015.row.b_who: Joint return + understatement from spouse + no knowledge + inequitable to hold liable
view.s6015.row.c_who: No longer married / separated 12+ mo; allocate as if MFS
view.s6015.row.f_who: Catch-all when (b)/(c) unavailable; Rev. Proc. 2013-34 weighing factors
view.s6015.warning.not_joint: Innocent spouse relief only applies to joint returns. Use Form 1040-X to amend or consider Equitable Relief if Form 8857 still timely.
view.s6033.dl.3_consecutive: 3 consecutive years failure: AUTOMATIC LOSS of exempt status
view.s6033.dl.990n: Form 990-N: filed online via IRS e-Postcard portal (free)
view.s6033.dl.extension: Form 8868 extension: 6-month automatic extension to November 15 (calendar)
view.s6033.dl.late_penalty: Late penalty: $20/day (up to $11K) or $100/day for large orgs (up to $56K)
view.s6033.dl.public_inspection: Public inspection: Forms 990 + 1023 available 3 yrs free at IRS
view.s6033.dl.reinstatement: Reinstatement via Form 1023 + Rev. Proc. 2014-11 streamlined process
view.s6033.dl.standard: Standard deadline: 15th day of 5th month after fiscal year end (May 15 for calendar)
view.s6033.exc.affiliate_of_church: Integrated auxiliary of church (e.g., school): NOT required to file (Form 4720 exception)
view.s6033.exc.below_minimum: Below $50K + first year: may still file Form 990-N for transparency
view.s6033.exc.exclusively_religious: Exclusively religious instructional orgs: NOT required to file
view.s6033.exc.govt_instrumentalities: Governmental units / instrumentalities: NOT required to file
view.s6033.exc.religious_orders: Religious orders / mission societies: NOT required to file
view.s6033.exc.unincorporated: Unincorporated 501(c)(3) under religious affiliation: may not need to file
view.s6033.exc.us_possessions: US possession affiliated orgs: limited filing
view.s6033.h1.title: // § 6033 EXEMPT ORG FILING
view.s6033.hint.intro: 501(c) orgs must file annual return: Form 990, 990-EZ, 990-N, or 990-PF. Form selection based on gross receipts + total assets. Form 990-N (e-Postcard): ≤ $50K gross receipts. Form 990-EZ: < $200K receipts + < $500K assets. Form 990 (full): ≥ $200K OR ≥ $500K. Form 990-PF: ALL private foundations. Failure 3 consecutive years: AUTOMATIC LOSS of exempt status (§ 6033(j) — Pension Protection Act 2006). Public inspection: Forms 990 publicly available.
view.s6033.revoked_note: AUTOMATIC REVOCATION: 3 consecutive years of failure to file. Exempt status LOST. Apply for reinstatement via Form 1023 + Rev. Proc. 2014-11 (streamlined available if eligible). Retroactive reinstatement possible if applied within 15 months of revocation notice. Public IRS list 'Auto Revocation' shows revoked orgs.
view.s6033.sb.501c3: 501(c)(3) public charity: only IRS sees names + addresses (post-2018 Rev. Proc. 2018-38)
view.s6033.sb.501c3_pi: Public inspection: amounts only (not donor names) for 501(c)(3) public charities
view.s6033.sb.501c4: 501(c)(4) + (5) + (6): NOT required (donor anonymity post-2018 Rev. Proc.)
view.s6033.sb.amat_v_irs: Americans for Prosperity v. Bonta (2021): SCOTUS struck down CA/NY donor disclosure
view.s6033.sb.federal_continue: Federal disclosure to IRS (not public): continues regardless
view.s6033.sb.pf: Private foundations: full disclosure even publicly (different rule)
view.s6033.sb.purpose: Discloses substantial contributors (≥ $5K AND ≥ 2% of total)
view.s6033.sb.state_rules: State-level: NY + CA stopped requiring after AFP v. Bonta
view.s6038.cat.1: US shareholder ≥ 10% in foreign passive investment company (§ 1297 PFIC overlap with 8621)
view.s6038.cat.2: US officer / director of foreign corp + US person owning ≥ 10%
view.s6038.cat.3: US person acquires ≥ 10% stock OR additional 5% during year
view.s6038.cat.5: US shareholder owning ≥ 10% of CFC at year end
view.s6038.h1.title: // § 6038 FORM 5471 CFC REPORTING
view.s6038.hint.intro: US person owning ≥ 10% of a CFC (foreign corp where US owners hold > 50%) must file Form 5471 with their return. Penalty: $10,000 per year per CFC for non-filing, escalating $10k/month up to $50k. Continuing failure reduces foreign tax credit. Subpart F + GILTI income flows through annually regardless of cash distribution. 5 categories of filer by ownership / control. Common trader trap: foreign holding companies.
view.s6038.label.name: Foreign corp name
view.s6038.stream.body: If you missed Form 5471 filings: Streamlined Domestic Offshore Procedures may allow late filing with reduced/waived penalties IF non-willful. Required: 3 years amended returns + 6 years FBARs + 5% offshore-account penalty (Domestic version). Foreign Streamlined: no penalty if non-resident. Voluntary Disclosure Program if willful: penalty up to 50% of high balance.
view.s6038.th.name: Name
view.s6038a.dis.body: Pre-2017: foreign-owned SMLLC = no US tax / filing if no ETB. Treas. Reg. § 301.7701-2 (final 2017) treats disregarded entity as separate corp for § 6038A reporting. Now required to file pro-forma 1120 + Form 5472. Doing nothing = $25k+ penalties per year.
view.s6038a.h1.title: // § 6038A FORM 5472 FOREIGN-OWNED US
view.s6038a.hint.intro: Required when (1) US corp with 25%+ foreign ownership, OR (2) foreign-owned single-member LLC (since 2017 disregarded entity rule). Reports related-party transactions in 11 categories. Penalty: $25,000 per failure (was $10k pre-2018) + $25k/month after 90 days. Common trap: foreign-owned US LLC holding US real estate. Pro-forma 1120 + Form 5472 required even for disregarded entity.
view.s6038a.rp.commonality: U.S. + foreign affiliates under common control
view.s6038a.rp.controlled: Any other foreign person controlled by 25% shareholder
view.s6038a.rp.cousins: Foreign brother-sister corps under common ownership
view.s6038a.rp.direct_25: Any 25% direct or indirect foreign shareholder
view.s6038a.rp.related_to_25: Any party related to 25% foreign shareholder under § 318 attribution
view.s6038a.warning.dis: Foreign-owned disregarded LLC (since 2017): MUST file pro-forma 1120 with Form 5472 attached. Common trap with foreign-owned single-member LLCs holding US real estate. Penalties stack year-over-year. No estate-tax / income filing history? Still required. File via 'Form 5472 only' return paperwork.
view.s6038b.form.5472: Form 5472: foreign-owned US corp / disregarded entity (§ 6038A)
view.s6038b.h1.title: // § 6038B FORM 926 FOREIGN TRANSFER
view.s6038b.hint.intro: US person transferring > $100k cash OR any property to foreign corp must file Form 926. Penalty: 10% of FMV (max $100k per failure; uncapped if intentional). § 367(a): US-built appreciation on transferred property triggers gain (foreign corp can't be tax shelter). § 367(d): intangibles get special 'deemed royalty' treatment. § 367(b): outbound reorganizations / spin-offs.
view.s6038d.h1.title: // § 6038D FORM 8938 SPECIFIED FOREIGN ASSETS
view.s6038d.hint.intro: Form 8938 reports SFFA when value exceeds threshold. $50K-$600K depending on status + abroad. $10K base + $10K/30-day continuation. § 6662(j) 40% accuracy.
view.s6039.espp.discount: 15% maximum discount on lower of: FMV at grant OR FMV at purchase
view.s6039.espp.disqualifying_treatment: Disqualifying disposition: discount + spread = ordinary income
view.s6039.espp.eligible: All 5%+ owner OR HCE-excluded employees may be excluded
view.s6039.espp.grant_holding: 2 years from beginning of offering period
view.s6039.espp.lookback: Lookback feature: discount based on lower of two prices
view.s6039.espp.qualifying_treatment: Qualifying disposition: discount = ordinary income up to gain, balance = LTCG
view.s6039.espp.s25k: $25,000 limit per offering period (FMV at grant basis)
view.s6039.espp.tax_form: W-2 reports compensation income on qualifying disposition
view.s6039.h1.title: // § 6039 ISO/ESPP INFORMATION REPORTING
view.s6039.hint.intro: Form 3921 (ISO) + Form 3922 (ESPP). Furnish to employee by Jan 31, file with IRS by Feb 28 paper / Mar 31 e-file. § 6721/6722 penalties up to $3.78M.
view.s6039.iso.amt_basis: AMT basis adjustment: increase by spread → reduces future AMT gain
view.s6039.iso.amt_credit: § 53 minimum tax credit allows future recovery of AMT paid
view.s6039.iso.amt_trap: AMT trap: § 56(b)(3) — spread at exercise included in AMTI
view.s6039.iso.both: BOTH must be satisfied for qualifying disposition
view.s6039.iso.disqual: Disqualifying disposition: ordinary income (FMV-strike) up to gain
view.s6039.iso.s100k: $100K rule: first $100K ISO grant per year eligible (excess = NSO)
view.s6039.iso.tax_qual: Tax treatment: 100% long-term capital gain on sale
view.s6039.tbl.aftaug1: Filed after Aug 1 or not at all
view.s6039.tbl.aug1: Filed after 30 days but by Aug 1
view.s6041.bw.b_notice: B-Notice / C-Notice: IRS sends to payer when discrepancy detected
view.s6041.bw.penalty: Payer penalty: 100% of unwithheld amount + interest if failure
view.s6041.bw.rate: Rate: 24% on REPORTABLE PAYMENT (since 2018 TCJA reduction from 28%)
view.s6041.bw.recipient_credit: Recipient credits BW against income tax on 1040 / 1120
view.s6041.bw.remit_945: Remit via Form 945 (Annual Return of Withheld Federal Income Tax)
view.s6041.bw.report_box4: Report on Form 1099 Box 4 (Federal Income Tax Withheld)
view.s6041.bw.triggers: Triggers: (1) no TIN provided, (2) incorrect TIN, (3) IRS notification (B-Notice)
view.s6041.bw.w9: Solution: get Form W-9 (Request for Taxpayer Identification Number)
view.s6041.bw_note: BACKUP WITHHOLDING REQUIRED — no W-9 / TIN from recipient. Withhold 24% from each payment + remit via Form 945 + report Box 4 of 1099. Recipient claims credit on income tax return. Failure to withhold: payer personally liable for entire amount. Obtain W-9 IMMEDIATELY before next payment.
view.s6041.dl.correction: Correction: file CORR 1099 promptly when error discovered
view.s6041.dl.efile_threshold: E-file mandatory: 10+ returns aggregate (post-2024 IRS regs)
view.s6041.dl.extension: Extension: Form 8809 (30 days automatic, may extend 30 more)
view.s6041.dl.misc_recipient: 1099-MISC: to recipient by January 31 (Boxes 8, 10 — by February 15)
view.s6041.exc.attorneys: EXCEPT: attorney fees (always reportable, even to law firm corp)
view.s6041.exc.corp_default: Corporations generally NOT subject to § 6041 reporting
view.s6041.exc.federal_govt: EXCEPT: federal executive agencies + federal contractors
view.s6041.exc.llc: LLCs: depends on TIN type (corp = exempt; partnership = reportable)
view.s6041.exc.s_corp: S-corps + LLCs (electing corp status): generally treated as corps
view.s6041.h1.title: // § 6041 INFO REPORTING (1099)
view.s6041.hint.intro: Payers must report $600+ payments to non-corp persons for services (NEC), rent, prizes, royalties (MISC). Threshold: $600 / yr per recipient. Form 1099-NEC (Box 1 nonemployee compensation) — since PATH Act 2020 separate from MISC. Form 1099-MISC (Boxes 1-14: rent, royalties, prizes, medical, attorney fees, etc.). Corporations exempt EXCEPT: attorneys, medical providers, federally-regulated. Backup withholding 24% if no TIN. Form 1096 transmittal.
view.s6041.pen.de_minimis: De minimis: 10 or less incorrect forms (or 1% of total) — no penalty
view.s6041.pen.intentional: Intentional disregard: $660 per form + UNLIMITED (no cap)
view.s6041.pen.reasonable_cause: Reasonable cause exception: Boyle case strict (Form 1099 disputes rare)
view.s6041.pen.s6722: § 6722 statement to payee: similar penalties for failure to furnish
view.s6041.pen.small_biz: Small biz ($5M or less): reduced caps ($79K / $239K / $479K)
view.s6041.pen.tier1: Tier 1: $60 per form if corrected within 30 days (max $230K)
view.s6041.pen.tier2: Tier 2: $120 per form if corrected by August 1 (max $683K)
view.s6041.pen.tier3: Tier 3: $330 per form if NOT corrected by August 1 (max $1.37M)
view.s6045.bm.average_cost: Average cost: mutual funds only — historical default; locked once elected
view.s6045.bm.dividend_reinvestment: Dividend reinvestment: each share treated as separate lot
view.s6045.bm.election_method: Election made AT or BEFORE sale (per share lot) — broker confirms in writing
view.s6045.bm.fifo_default: FIFO: oldest lots sold first — DEFAULT for stocks if no election
view.s6045.bm.hifo: HIFO: tax-loss harvesting (highest basis first to maximize loss / minimize gain)
view.s6045.bm.lifo: LIFO: newest lots first (uncommon for equities, common bonds)
view.s6045.bm.specific_id: Specific identification: choose specific lot — REQUIRES BROKER CONFIRMATION at sale (settlement date)
view.s6045.bm.tax_lot_optimization: Tax lot optimization: most brokers offer auto-HIFO / 'max tax loss' mode
view.s6045.box_b_note: Box B (basis NOT reported to IRS): taxpayer must independently track + report basis. Common for: pre-2011 stock acquisitions, ESPP / RSU shares (often broker basis incorrect), inherited / gifted shares, transfers between brokers. Maintain personal records — IRS may audit if numbers diverge from broker reporting.
view.s6045.cov.bonds_2014: Less complex bonds: acquired AFTER 1/1/2014 — covered
view.s6045.cov.box_a: Box A: short-term basis reported to IRS
view.s6045.cov.box_b: Box B: short-term, basis NOT reported (taxpayer responsibility)
view.s6045.cov.box_d: Box D: long-term basis reported to IRS
view.s6045.cov.box_e: Box E: long-term, basis NOT reported
view.s6045.cov.complex_2016: Complex debt + options: acquired AFTER 1/1/2016 — covered
view.s6045.cov.digital_2025: Digital assets: brokers report effective 2025 (Form 1099-DA + Form 1099-DA-G)
view.s6045.cov.mutual_fund_2012: Mutual funds + ETFs: acquired AFTER 1/1/2012 — covered
view.s6045.cov.uncovered: Uncovered: pre-effective-date acquisitions OR transferred from non-broker
view.s6045.h1.title: // § 6045 BROKER REPORTING
view.s6045.hint.intro: Broker must report sales of securities + BASIS information to IRS + customer via Form 1099-B. Covered securities (since 2011): equities, mutual funds, options, futures, debt instruments. Basis methods: FIFO (default), LIFO, specific identification, average cost (mutual funds). Wash sale + dividend reinvestment + corporate action adjustments included. Box B: basis NOT reported to IRS (legacy / uncovered). § 6045(g): digital asset broker reporting (post-2023 Form 1099-DA).
view.s6045.recon.basis_difference: Basis difference from 1099-B: report adjustments + explanation
view.s6045.recon.code: Code column: adjustments codes (W wash sale, B basis adj, T term adj, etc.)
view.s6045.recon.cross_reference: Cross-reference: 1099-B box totals to Schedule D line totals
view.s6045.recon.estate_step_up: Inherited: § 1014 step-up to date-of-death FMV (often higher basis)
view.s6045.recon.gift: Gift: § 1015 carryover basis + dual basis rule
view.s6045.recon.schedule_d: Schedule D: net gain / loss from Form 8949
view.s6045.ws.30_days: Wash sale: buy 'substantially identical' within 30 days before / after sale at loss
view.s6045.ws.crypto: Crypto: NO wash sale rule currently (proposed legislation)
view.s6045.ws.disallowed: Loss DISALLOWED + ADDED to basis of replacement shares
view.s6045.ws.holding_period: Holding period of replacement shares TACKS from disallowed sale
view.s6045.ws.options_replacement: Option grant to buy same security: triggers wash sale
view.s6045.ws.same_broker_account: Within same account only — IRS Rev. Rul. 2008-5 (across accounts also)
view.s6045.ws.short_sales: Short sale closure triggers similar 30-day rule (Rev. Rul. 60-159)
view.s6045.ws.spouse_iras: Cross-account: IRA / 401(k) / spouse account also can trigger
view.s6045a.h1.title: // § 6045A COVERED SECURITY TRANSFER REPORTING
view.s6045a.hint.intro: Transferring broker must furnish basis statement to receiving broker within 15 days. Covered security phase-in 2011-2016. § 6722 penalty $60-$630/form.
view.s6045b.act.cash_in_lieu: Cash in lieu of fractional shares
view.s6045b.act.stock_split: Stock splits, reverse splits, stock dividends > 5%
view.s6045b.broker.constructive: § 1259 constructive sale: separate broker reporting category
view.s6045b.broker.cov_uncov: 'Covered' vs 'non-covered' securities — only covered have basis reported
view.s6045b.broker.elect_default: Default cost method: FIFO; can elect HIFO / specific ID before sale
view.s6045b.broker.short_sale: Short sale basis: reporting begins year position closes
view.s6045b.broker.transfer: Transferring broker must send Form 1099-B + transfer statement within 15 days
view.s6045b.broker.wash_sale: Brokers must report wash-sale adjustments to basis
view.s6045b.cov.crypto_future: Crypto / digital assets: post-2025 reporting effective per IRA + IRS rules
view.s6045b.cov.dividends: Debt securities acquired after 1/1/2014 + complex debt after 1/1/2016
view.s6045b.cov.mutual_fund: Mutual fund + DRIP shares acquired after 1/1/2012
view.s6045b.h1.title: // § 6045B FORM 8937 COST BASIS
view.s6045b.hint.intro: Issuers must file Form 8937 within 45 days of any 'specified action affecting basis' — spinoffs, mergers, splits, mandatory exchanges. Public on issuer website. Brokers (§ 6045) report adjusted basis on 1099-B (covered stocks post-2011, mutual funds post-2012). Penalty: $310 per failure (2024); $630 if intentional. Annual cap: $3.78M. § 6045B-1(d) safe harbor: posting on website with 30-day notice to holders.
view.s6045b.label.notice: Notice provided to holders within 15 days?
view.s6048.excl.canadian_rrsp: Canadian RRSP / RRIF (Rev. Proc. 2014-55 auto exclusion)
view.s6048.excl.medical_education: Direct tuition / medical payments by foreign donor
view.s6048.excl.qualified_employee_trust: Qualified employee benefit trust (separate Form 8891 / FBAR)
view.s6048.h1.title: // § 6048 FORM 3520 FOREIGN TRUST / GIFT
view.s6048.hint.intro: Three reportable events: (1) transfer to foreign trust; (2) distribution from foreign trust; (3) receipt of > $100,000 gift from foreign individual / estate, OR > $19,570 (2024) from foreign corp / partnership. Penalty: 35% of unreported amount (5%/month, min $10k). Form 3520-A annually by trustee. U.S. owner of foreign grantor trust reports trust income on 1040 even if no distribution.
view.s6048.relief.delinquent: Delinquent International Information Return Submission Procedures (DIIRSP)
view.s6048.relief.precluded: Quiet disclosure / amended return — NOT penalty relief
view.s6048.relief.streamlined_dom: Streamlined Domestic Offshore (5% Title 26 miscellaneous penalty)
view.s6049.box.14: Box 14: tax-exempt + tax credit bond CUSIP
view.s6049.box.2: Box 2: early withdrawal penalty (deductible on Schedule 1)
view.s6049.box.9: Box 9: specified private activity bond interest (AMT preference)
view.s6049.exem.bank_dep_nra: Bank deposits to NRAs: § 871(i) exemption from 30% withholding (since 2013); still reported via 1042-S
view.s6049.exem.muni_exempt: Tax-exempt entities: NOT EXEMPT from reporting (but they still get 1099-INT)
view.s6049.exem.portfolio_int: § 871(h) portfolio interest: 0% withholding for foreign payees
view.s6049.exem.tin: Less than $10: no reporting (Box 1 + Box 3 combined)
view.s6049.exem.under_10: Threshold: Box 1 + Box 3 combined ≥ $10 → must file
view.s6049.h1.title: // § 6049 INTEREST REPORTING (1099-INT)
view.s6049.hint.intro: Payers must report $10+ interest paid + $600+ in trade/business interest. Form 1099-INT: Box 1 (interest income), Box 2 (early withdrawal penalty), Box 3 (US gov interest), Box 4 (federal withholding), Box 8 (tax-exempt interest), Box 9 (private activity bond interest — AMT prefer item). Form 1099-OID separate for original issue discount. Foreign payees: Form 1042-S separate regime (with W-8BEN). Bank deposit interest to NRAs: § 871(i) exemption from withholding (since 2013).
view.s6049.oid.box1: 1099-OID Box 1: OID amount accrued in current year
view.s6049.oid.de_minimis: De minimis OID: 0.25% × years to maturity → not reportable
view.s6049.oid.separate: OID accrual reported on separate Form 1099-OID, not 1099-INT
view.s6049.oid.short_term: Short-term obligations ≤ 1 year: no OID reporting
view.s6049.oid.tax_exempt: Tax-exempt municipal OID: excluded similar to regular muni interest
view.s6049.oid.taxpayer_pays: Taxpayer pays tax on accrual EVEN WITHOUT cash interest (zero-coupon problem)
view.s6049.oid.zero_coupon: Zero-coupon bonds: large OID accrual + no cash interest payments
view.s6049.pab_amt_note: Private Activity Bond (Box 9): federal tax-exempt for regular tax BUT AMT preference item adds back to AMTI. Watch out: high-income taxpayers w/ tax-exempt PAB income may unexpectedly hit AMT. Consider swapping to non-PAB muni bonds if AMT triggers consistently.
view.s6049.te.alternative_minimum: Pre-2018 individual AMT preference; post-2025 sunset uncertain
view.s6049.te.amt_pab: AMT preference: private activity bond interest (Box 9) — adds back for AMT
view.s6049.te.aotc: Tax-exempt interest affects: Social Security taxation, AOTC phaseout, IRA contribution limits
view.s6049.te.federal: Federal: municipal bond interest (Box 8) excluded from gross income
view.s6049.te.modified_agi: Modified AGI for ACA premium credit: includes tax-exempt interest
view.s6049.te.state_in_state: In-state municipal bonds: usually state tax exempt too
view.s6049.te.state_out_state: Out-of-state municipal: federal exempt + state TAXABLE (most states)
view.s6049.te.treasury_state: US Treasury / agency: state tax exempt (Box 3)
view.s6050W.exc.charity: Charitable contributions: excluded from PSE reporting (donor letter substantiates)
view.s6050W.exc.combined: Aggregation: PSE aggregates ALL transactions under SAME TIN/account
view.s6050W.exc.foreign: Foreign payees: not reportable (different reporting under FATCA / Ch 3 / 4)
view.s6050W.exc.personal: Personal transfers (gifts, splitting bills): NOT reportable; PSE must distinguish
view.s6050W.exc.refunds: Refunds + chargebacks: GROSS — taxpayer reconciles to net on Schedule C
view.s6050W.exc.s139_disaster: Disaster relief payments (§ 139): excluded from gross income, may still appear
view.s6050W.exc.tax_exempt: Tax-exempt entities: still receive 1099-K but typically excluded from UBI
view.s6050W.exc.under_threshold: Below threshold: PSE may STILL report (de minimis) — many file anyway
view.s6050W.h1.title: // § 6050W 1099-K REPORTING
view.s6050W.hint.intro: PSE (Payment Settlement Entity) reports gross payments via Form 1099-K. ARPA 2021 threshold $600 — Notice 2023-74 + 2024-85 delayed phase-in: $20K + 200 txns (2023, 2024 baseline) → $5K (2024) → $2.5K (2025) → $600 (2026). Affects: PayPal, Venmo, Cash App, Etsy, eBay, Stripe, Square, Airbnb sellers. Gross only — no expense deduction on form. Reconcile on Schedule C / Form 8949.
view.s6050W.personal_note: Personal transfers (friends + family): NOT taxable income. Reconcile on Schedule 1 — report Other Income gross then reverse with Other Adjustment 'Form 1099-K personal transfers not reportable'. PSE should have flagged as 'Personal Payment' but often misclassified.
view.s6050W.rec.airbnb: Airbnb / VRBO: Schedule E if not service business; Schedule C if substantial services
view.s6050W.rec.crypto: Crypto exchanges 1099-K: report on Form 8949 (specific identification by lot)
view.s6050W.rec.disagree: Disagree with PSE? Request CORR from PSE → Form 1040 reconciliation statement
view.s6050W.rec.friends: Friends + family Venmo: NOT income — but document and exclude on Schedule 1 adj
view.s6050W.rec.gig: Gig income: Schedule C if regular trade or Schedule SE for self-employment tax
view.s6050W.rec.personal_gain: Personal item GAIN: Schedule D — collectibles gain (Form 8949 capital gain)
view.s6050W.rec.personal_loss: Personal item LOSS (e.g., sold used couch): Schedule 1 → 'Other Income +X' then 'Other Adj -X'
view.s6050W.rec.schedule_c: Business income: Schedule C — gross 1099-K + COGS + expenses → net profit
view.s6051.box.1: Box 1: Wages, tips, other compensation (subject to federal income tax)
view.s6051.box.12: Box 12: Various codes (see list — most common)
view.s6051.box.13: Box 13: Statutory employee / retirement plan / third-party sick pay
view.s6051.box.3: Box 3: Social Security wages (up to wage base $176,100 for 2025)
view.s6051.dp.extension: Extension: Form 8809 (30-day automatic; only available if hardship)
view.s6051.dp.s6722: § 6722 penalty: failure to furnish to employee — same tiers
view.s6051.dp.ssa: Due to SSA: January 31 (paper + electronic) since 2017 PATH Act
view.s6051.dp.w3: Form W-3 transmittal: submit with paper W-2; not required for SSA electronic
view.s6051.h1.title: // § 6051 W-2 WAGE STATEMENTS
view.s6051.hint.intro: Employers furnish W-2 Wage + Tax Statement to each employee + Social Security Administration. Box 1: federal wages (taxable). Box 3 + 5: Social Security + Medicare wages. Box 12: codes for retirement (D, E, G), HSA (W), dep care (DD), group life > $50K (C), etc. Form W-3 transmittal. Due January 31 (employee + SSA, regardless of paper/electronic). SSA matching: wages cross-check vs SSA records.
view.s6051.label.s_corp_premium: S-corp health premium added to Box 1 ($)
view.s6051.pre.401k: 401(k) / 403(b) elective deferrals: reduce Box 1 but NOT Box 3/5 (FICA still applies)
view.s6051.pre.dep_care: Dependent care FSA: reduces Box 1 + Box 3 + Box 5 ($5K limit 2025)
view.s6051.pre.health: § 125 health insurance premiums: reduce Box 1 + Box 3 + Box 5 (full pre-tax)
view.s6051.pre.health_pre_tax_vs_post: After-tax health insurance: does NOT reduce — paid from net pay
view.s6051.pre.hsa: HSA contributions: reduce Box 1 + Box 3 + Box 5 (full pre-tax)
view.s6051.pre.imputed_income: Imputed income (group life > $50K): ADDS to Box 1 + Box 3 + Box 5
view.s6051.pre.transit: Transit / parking: reduces Box 1 + Box 3 + Box 5 ($325/mo each 2025)
view.s6051.scorp.box_14: Optionally disclosed in Box 14 with code 'S CORP HEALTH'
view.s6051.scorp.deduction: Owner takes § 162(l) above-the-line deduction on personal return
view.s6051.scorp.health_taxable: Health insurance premiums INCLUDED in Box 1 (TAXABLE wages)
view.s6051.scorp.hsa: HSA: similar rule — included in Box 1, but excluded from Box 3/5 if HDHP
view.s6051.scorp.k1_split: Net effect: tax-free for FICA but income tax-free via § 162(l)
view.s6051.scorp.life_insurance: Group term life: similar inclusion rules
view.s6051.scorp.no_fica: Health premiums NOT in Box 3/5 (NO FICA on this portion)
view.s6051.scorp_note: 2%+ S-corp owner: health insurance premiums INCLUDED in Box 1 wages. NO FICA on health portion (Box 3/5 reduced). Owner takes § 162(l) above-the-line deduction on Form 1040. Net result: full income deductibility WITHOUT FICA. Box 14 disclosure recommended: 'S CORP HEALTH $X' for clarity.
view.s6072.ext.abroad: Abroad: automatic 2-month extension to June 15 — file by then OR file 4868 for additional 4 months
view.s6072.ext.combat: Combat zone: extension to 180 days after leaving zone
view.s6072.ext.disaster: Federally declared disaster: IRS announces specific extended dates per region
view.s6072.ext.entity: Entity: Form 7004 by original due date → automatic 6-month (5.5 for trust)
view.s6072.ext.individual: Individual: Form 4868 by April 15 → automatic 6-month extension to Oct 15
view.s6072.ext.no_combo: Extension does NOT extend SE tax payment due date
view.s6072.ext.pay_required: CRITICAL: extension to FILE only, NOT to pay — interest + penalty start from original due date
view.s6072.h1.title: // § 6072 RETURN DUE DATES
view.s6072.hint.intro: Individual: April 15 (calendar). C-corp: 15th day of 4th month after year-end. Partnership + S-corp: 15th day of 3rd month after year-end (March 15 calendar). Trust / estate: 15th day of 4th month (April 15 calendar). Extension: 6-month automatic via Form 4868 (individual) / Form 7004 (entities). Extension to FILE — NOT to PAY. Combat zone: 6+ months. Abroad: June 15 automatic for individuals.
view.s6072.late.6651a1: § 6651(a)(1) failure to file: 5% / month up to 25% of unpaid tax
view.s6072.late.6651a2: § 6651(a)(2) failure to pay: 0.5% / month up to 25% of unpaid tax
view.s6072.late.collection_alternative: Collection alternatives: installment agreement, OIC, CNC
view.s6072.late.first_time: First Time Penalty Abatement (FTA): one-time relief if compliant 3 prior yrs
view.s6072.late.interest: Interest at federal short-term rate + 3% (compounded daily)
view.s6072.late.minimum: Minimum failure-to-file penalty: smaller of $485 (2024) or 100% of unpaid tax
view.s6072.late.reasonable_cause: Reasonable cause exception: Boyle case very strict (limited to extraordinary circumstances)
view.s6072.unpaid_note: Unpaid balance + no extension: failure-to-file PLUS failure-to-pay penalties accrue from original due date. Combined penalty up to 47.5%. File ASAP even if can't pay. Form 9465 installment agreement available. First Time Penalty Abatement may waive if compliant 3 prior years.
view.s6111.cat.confidential: Confidential — fee paid + restriction on disclosure
view.s6111.cat.contractual: Contractual protection — refund if no tax benefit
view.s6111.cat.toi: Transactions of Interest — IRS designates as warranting scrutiny
view.s6111.coord.disclosure_taxpayer: Strategy disclosure may trigger client wave of audit + § 6662
view.s6111.coord.eited_taxpayer: Even if advisor fails to file 8918, taxpayer still required 8886
view.s6111.coord.injunction: § 7408 injunction available against repeat material advisor
view.s6111.coord.list_extension: List request triggers attorney-supervisor review at IRS national office
view.s6111.coord.sol_exception: § 6501(c)(10) extended SOL: 1 year after Form 8886 filed
view.s6111.coord.taxpayer: Taxpayer separately files Form 8886 (§ 6011) attached to return
view.s6111.h1.title: // § 6111 MATERIAL ADVISOR DISCLOSURE
view.s6111.hint.intro: Material advisor of reportable transaction must file Form 8918. § 6707 penalty: $50K (other) OR greater of $200K OR 75% gross income (listed/intentional). § 6112 list maintenance $10K/day.
view.s6111.list.contents: List: name, address, TIN, amount invested, copy of advice, all fees
view.s6111.list.no_cap: NO cap on total — can exceed $200K easily
view.s6111.list.penalty_clock: $10,000/day starts on day 21 — continues until full production
view.s6111.list.privilege: Attorney-client privilege NOT general defense — § 7525 narrow
view.s6111.list.production: Must produce within 20 business days of IRS written request
view.s6111.list.required: Material advisor must maintain list of advisees + transactions
view.s6111.list.retention: 7-year retention period from date last became material advisor
view.s6111.tbl.listed_tp: 75% of decrease in tax / $200K cap / $100K min
view.s6111.tbl.other_tp: 75% of decrease in tax / $50K cap / $10K min
view.s6111.thresh.cumulative: Cumulative across multiple participants in same transaction
view.s6111.thresh.expectation: Reasonably expects to receive — not actually received
view.s6111.thresh.listed_natural: Listed transaction, natural person: $50,000 gross income
view.s6112.h1.title: // § 6112 MATERIAL ADVISOR LIST MAINTENANCE
view.s6112.hint.intro: Material advisor must maintain advisee list 7 years + produce within 20 business days of IRS request. § 6708 $10K/day penalty with NO CAP.
view.s6159.def.csed_runs: IA running CSED restarts at termination
view.s6159.def.didnt_provide: Failed to provide requested financial info
view.s6159.def.estimated: Falls behind on estimated tax payments
view.s6159.def.new_balance: Owed new tax balance + didn't pay
view.s6159.h1.title: // § 6159 INSTALLMENT AGREEMENT
view.s6159.hint.intro: Pay debt monthly. Guaranteed IA: < $10k, 36 mo, automatic. Streamlined: < $50k, 72 mo. Routine: < $250k (post-2020). Non-streamlined: > $50k requires Form 433-F. Partial Pay IA (PPIA): reduced payment runs out CSED; debt forgiven at CSED. Setup fee: $22-$178 (waived low-income). Failure to pay = default.
view.s6166.acc.50_pct: ≥ 50% of qualifying business disposed of or withdrawn
view.s6166.acc.distribution: Undistributed net income retained (any amount triggers under § 6166(g)(2))
view.s6166.acc.distribution_corp: Corporate redemptions to pay estate tax (§ 303 carve-out limited)
view.s6166.acc.late: Late payment of installment after 6-month grace
view.s6166.h1.title: // § 6166 ESTATE TAX INSTALLMENT
view.s6166.hint.intro: Up to 14 years to pay federal estate tax on closely-held business interest. Years 1-4: interest-only at favorable 2% rate (on first $1.85M tax, 2024). Years 5-14: P+I. Closely-held = > 35% of adjusted gross estate must be active trade or business. Acceleration if 50% of business sold / undistributed income / late payment. Lien required; Form 706 election.
view.s6166.qual.aggregate: Aggregate test: combine multiple businesses (each ≥ 20% required)
view.s6166.qual.farm_business: Farms, ranches, family agricultural operations qualify
view.s6166.qual.holding_companies: Holding companies: must look through to operating sub
view.s6213.h1.title: // § 6213 NOTICE OF DEFICIENCY
view.s6213.hint.intro: IRS must issue 'Notice of Deficiency (90-day letter)' before assessment. 90 days to file Tax Court petition (150 days outside US). Petition SUSPENDS assessment + collection. Miss deadline = lose Tax Court rights. Alternative paths: pay + sue for refund (District Court / Court of Federal Claims) OR enter Appeals (administrative).
view.s6213.strat.appeals_first: Always try IRS Appeals BEFORE Tax Court (no cost, can settle)
view.s6213.strat.designated: Designated payment under § 6603 stops interest from running
view.s6213.strat.district_pros: District Court PROS: jury option, better for 'ordinary law' issues
view.s6213.strat.last_known_address: Notice valid if mailed to 'last known address' — even if returned
view.s6213.strat.payment_during: Payment during petition recharacterizes as deposit, refunded if win
view.s6213.strat.precedent: Choose forum based on most favorable PRECEDENT (DC Circuit favors gov't)
view.s6213.strat.statute_running: Petition timing = day 90 of notice (postmark counts for first-class mail)
view.s6213.strat.tax_court_pros: Tax Court PROS: don't pay first, suspend collection, expertise, small-case option
view.s6213.warning.urgent: URGENT: petition deadline approaching. File Tax Court petition NOW via TaxCourt.gov or pay deficiency + designate § 6603 deposit while pursuing claims.
view.s6221.h1.title: // § 6221 BBA PARTNERSHIP AUDIT REGIME
view.s6221.hint.intro: Post-2017 default: entity-level audit + 37% imputed underpayment. § 6221(b) election out for ≤ 100 eligible partners. § 6226 push-out election within 45 days.
view.s6321.h1.title: // § 6321 FEDERAL TAX LIEN
view.s6321.hint.intro: Federal tax lien arises AUTOMATICALLY at assessment for tax + penalty + interest. Does NOT require NFTL filing. NFTL (Notice of Federal Tax Lien) sets PUBLIC priority vs other secured creditors. Attaches to ALL property + rights to property (Drye doctrine). Affects credit score until 10-yr CSED expiration or release. § 6325 options: release, discharge (specific property), withdrawal (false), subordination.
view.s6321.opt.cdp: CDP hearing (§ 6320): right to challenge NFTL within 30 days of filing
view.s6321.opt.compromise: Offer-in-Compromise § 7122 settles debt for less + lien release
view.s6321.opt.discharge: Discharge specific property (§ 6325(b)): pay equity attributable to that property
view.s6321.opt.equivalent: Equivalent hearing: post-30-day window, less powerful
view.s6321.opt.fresh_start: 'Fresh Start' 2011: NFTL threshold raised to $10k; withdrawal after full pay
view.s6321.opt.release: Release (§ 6325(a)): full payment OR CSED expired OR bond posted
view.s6321.opt.subordination: Subordination (§ 6325(d)): IRS gives priority to another lender
view.s6321.opt.withdrawal: Withdrawal (§ 6323(j)): erroneous filing OR not in best interest OR direct debit agreement
view.s6321.pri.45_day: 45-day window after NFTL: existing secured creditor still has priority
view.s6321.pri.mechanics: Mechanics lien + materialman lien generally retain priority
view.s6321.pri.personal: Personal property: file with state designation (UCC-like)
view.s6321.pri.purchaser: 'Purchaser' / 'holder of security interest' before NFTL = priority
view.s6321.pri.real_property: Real property: file with county recorder where situated
view.s6321.pri.super_priority: 'Super-priority' exceptions: § 6323(b) — wages, attorney fees, casualty insurance, etc.
view.s6325.app.administrative: Administrative appeals: Office of Appeals review
view.s6325.app.cdp: § 6320 CDP hearing: 30 days from Notice of Lien Filing
view.s6325.app.collateral: Collateral attack via refund suit limited
view.s6325.app.equivalent: Equivalent hearing (post-30-day): less powerful but available 1 yr
view.s6325.app.form_12277: Form 12277 — withdraw notice if benefits taxpayer (compliance / OIC / IA)
view.s6325.app.s6326: § 6326: 5 days to appeal erroneous filing — admin review
view.s6325.app.tax_court: Tax Court appeal after Final Notice of Determination
view.s6325.app.tax_court_lien: Tax Court can issue determination on lien filing appropriate
view.s6325.coll.s6321: § 6321: tax lien — attaches to all property + rights
view.s6325.coll.s6322: § 6322: lien arises at time of assessment
view.s6325.coll.s6323: § 6323: notice of lien — priority + protection for certain creditors
view.s6325.d.b1: § 6325(b)(1): RETAIN value ≥ 2× liability — value safely covers exposure
view.s6325.d.b2: § 6325(b)(2): VALUELESS to government — no IRS interest in property
view.s6325.d.b3: § 6325(b)(3): BOND or cash deposit — substitute security
view.s6325.d.process_time: Typical: 30-60 days to process discharge application
view.s6325.d.refinance: Refi: § 6325(d) subordination allows new lien priority
view.s6325.d.sale: Sale of property: IRS interest follows proceeds (Subordination)
view.s6325.delay_note: Request pending > 30 days. Contact IRS Centralized Lien Office (CLO) at 800-913-6050. Form 911 to Taxpayer Advocate Service if hardship. CDP hearing may be available if appeal rights not exhausted. Consider local TAS if facing financial harm.
view.s6325.h1.title: // § 6325 LIEN RELEASE
view.s6325.hint.intro: Lien RELEASED when: liability satisfied, uncollectible, OR bond posted. § 6325(a)(1): full payment / accepted Offer in Compromise. § 6325(b)(1): partial release of specific property — value retained ≥ 2× liability. § 6325(b)(2): discharge if government's interest valueless. § 6325(b)(3): bond / cash deposit. § 6325(d): subordination for refinancing. § 6326: 5-day appeal of erroneous filing.
view.s6325.r.bonded: Bond posted equal to liability (Form 12153 if appealing)
view.s6325.r.paid: Full payment of liability + interest + penalties
view.s6325.r.refile: Refile possible if facts change OR new period within statute
view.s6325.r.self_releasing: Self-releasing lien: certain liens release automatically (Form 668(Y))
view.s6325.r.timing: Release issued within 30 days of request / qualifying event
view.s6325.r.uncollectible: CNC (Currently Not Collectible) status + lien hindering collection
view.s6330.h1.title: // § 6330 CDP LEVY HEARING
view.s6330.hint.intro: 30-day notice before levy. Form 12153 within 30 days. Independent Appeals officer. Tax Court jurisdiction. Levy suspended + § 6502 collection SOL tolled.
view.s6331.ex.basic_living: Basic living standard (per IRS exemption tables)
view.s6331.ex.fuel: $13,510 (2024) of fuel + provisions + furniture + personal effects
view.s6331.ex.social_security: Social Security: only 15% can be levied (FPLP automated levy)
view.s6331.h1.title: // § 6331 LEVY / WAGE GARNISHMENT
view.s6331.hint.intro: Levy seizes property after notice + demand + Final Notice of Intent + 30 days. CDP right under § 6330: 30 days to request hearing → SUSPENDS collection. Exemptions: tools of trade ($5,540 2024), unemployment, child support, basic living standard. Continuous wage levy takes everything ABOVE exempt amount (Form 668-W). Bank levy is ONE-TIME (snapshot at receipt).
view.s6331.res.cdp: CDP hearing within 30 days of Final Notice (Form 12153)
view.s6331.res.equivalent: Equivalent hearing (post-30-day) Form 13753 — less powerful but available
view.s6331.res.release: Request levy release: Form 911 or Taxpayer Advocate
view.s6331.warning.cdp_urgent: URGENT: Final Notice (LT11) received. File Form 12153 within 30 days to request CDP hearing — preserves all collection alternatives + Tax Court review of any determination. Missing window means equivalent hearing only.
view.s6404.h1.title: // § 6404 ABATEMENT OF TAX / INTEREST / PENALTIES
view.s6404.hint.intro: § 6404(e) IRS delay 18+ mo + § 6404(f) erroneous written advice + § 6404(g) 36-mo notice failure suspension. Form 843. First-Time Penalty Abatement (FTA) IRM.
view.s6502.h1.title: // § 6502 CSED 10-YR COLLECTION SOL
view.s6502.hint.intro: IRS must collect within 10 years of assessment. After CSED: debt legally UNCOLLECTIBLE (lien auto-releases). Suspensions extend CSED: OIC pending, CDP hearing, bankruptcy (+ 6 mo), military combat, IA pending, Innocent Spouse application (+ 60 days each). Verify dates via Form 4340 (account transcript). Misjudgment by IRS happens — fight for the original CSED.
view.s6502.strat.cnc: Currently Not Collectible (CNC) status preserves CSED running
view.s6502.strat.fpa: File-then-pay: Get-out-of-jail-free if pay before CSED but it just expired
view.s6502.strat.no_borrow: Don't borrow money to pay near-CSED debt
view.s6502.strat.no_ia: DON'T enter IA in final year — extends CSED
view.s6502.strat.no_oic: DON'T file OIC just before CSED — extends collection time
view.s6502.strat.partial_pay: Partial-pay IA: CSED still runs, debt forgiven at CSED
view.s6502.strat.review_transcript: Review Form 4340 annually — IRS sometimes miscalculates CSED
view.s6502.strat.run_out_clock: Run out the clock: hold tight + comply with current filing requirements
view.s6502.toll.cdp: § 6320/§ 6330 CDP hearing: from CDP request through final determination + appeals
view.s6502.toll.ia: Installment agreement pending: from submission + 30 days after termination
view.s6502.toll.innocent_spouse: § 6015 Innocent Spouse election + 60 days after IRS final determination
view.s6502.toll.oic: § 7122 OIC pending: pendency + 30 days after rejection/withdrawal
view.s6502.toll.signed_extension: § 6502(a)(2) waiver extension signed by taxpayer (RARE; avoid)
view.s6601.h1.title: // § 6601 INTEREST ON UNDERPAYMENTS
view.s6601.hint.intro: Daily compound interest from due date. § 6621(a)(2) ST + 3pp underpayment / § 6621(c) hot corp ≥ $100K = ST + 5pp. § 6404(e) IRS-delay abatement.
view.s6651.h1.title: // § 6651 FAILURE TO FILE / PAY
view.s6651.hint.intro: (a)(1) Failure to file: 5%/month of unpaid tax, max 25%. Min $485 (2024) if > 60 days late. (a)(2) Failure to pay: 0.5%/month, max 25%. Combined: FTF rate REDUCED to 4.5%/month when both apply (so combined never exceeds 5%/month). FRAUDULENT FTF: 15%/month, max 75%. First-Time Abatement available for compliant taxpayers (penalty once / 3 yrs).
view.s6651.rel.disaster: Federally-declared disaster: automatic extension per IRS announcement
view.s6651.rel.fta: First-Time Abatement: clean 3-yr record + currently filing/paying compliance
view.s6651.rel.ia_ftp: FTP rate halved (0.25%/mo) during installment agreement period
view.s6651.rel.ignorance: Ignorance of law NOT reasonable cause (United States v. Boyle 1985)
view.s6651.rel.no_fta_fraud: FTA + reasonable cause NOT available for fraudulent FTF
view.s6651.rel.reasonable: Reasonable cause: serious illness, natural disaster, records destroyed, IRS error
view.s6651.rel2.6651_d: § 6651(d): rate INCREASED to 1%/month after IRS issues 10-day notice + levy threat
view.s6651.rel2.6651_h: § 6651(h): rate REDUCED to 0.25%/month during installment agreement
view.s6651.rel2.6656: § 6656 Failure to deposit (employer trust fund taxes — 2-15% by lateness)
view.s6651.rel2.6672: § 6672 Trust Fund Recovery Penalty (TFRP) — 100% of unpaid trust fund taxes
view.s6654.h1.title: // § 6654 ESTIMATED TAX SAFE HARBOR
view.s6654.hint.intro: Safe harbors avoid underpayment penalty: (1) 90% of current-year tax OR (2) 100% of prior-year tax (110% if prior AGI > $150k). Quarterly due: Apr 15, Jun 15, Sep 15, Jan 15. Withholding is treated as paid evenly across the year. Penalty = federal short-term AFR + 3% per quarter on each quarter's shortage. Farmers / fishermen: 66.67% safe harbor + Jan 15 single-date pay.
view.s6654.work.annualized: Form 2210 Schedule AI: annualized income method for lumpy / Q4-loaded income
view.s6654.work.farmer: Farmer / fisherman: pay 66.67% by Jan 15 OR full tax by Mar 1, no quarterly
view.s6654.work.first_year: First-year filer with no prior-year liability: no penalty
view.s6654.work.january: January 15 final due date — pay shortage then
view.s6654.work.withhold_dec: Increase W-2 withholding in December — treated as paid evenly across year
view.s6655.ann.elections: 3 different election periods available (months may vary)
view.s6655.ann.purpose: Purpose: avoid penalty when income SPIKED in later periods
view.s6655.ann.q2: Q2: annualize Jan-May × 12/5 → tax × 50%, minus Q1
view.s6655.ann.q3: Q3: annualize Jan-Aug × 12/8 → tax × 75%, minus Q1+Q2
view.s6655.ann.q4: Q4: annualize Jan-Nov × 12/11 → tax × 100%, minus Q1+Q2+Q3
view.s6655.ann.recalculation: Each quarter rechecked — late-year underpayment may trigger earlier penalty
view.s6655.annualized_note: Annualized income method elected: payment requirements based on YTD income annualized quarterly, not flat 25%. Particularly valuable for cyclical businesses w/ Q4 spikes. Form 2220 Part III computation. Reduces Q1-Q3 underpayment but Q4 cumulative test catches up if not paid sufficiently.
view.s6655.h1.title: // § 6655 CORP ESTIMATED TAX
view.s6655.hint.intro: Corp must pay estimated tax in 4 installments (4/15, 6/15, 9/15, 12/15) totaling 100% of expected tax. Underpayment penalty: federal short-term rate + 3% on shortfall (Form 2220). Safe harbors: (1) 100% of prior-year tax (large corp: 100% of CURRENT year), (2) annualized income method, (3) adjusted seasonal installment. Large corp: avg taxable income ≥ $1M in any of past 3 yrs — ONLY current-year safe harbor available. Form 1120-W worksheet (not filed).
view.s6655.pen.exception_first: First-year corp + small operations: no penalty under § 6655(g)
view.s6655.pen.from: Period: due date of installment to earlier of payment OR Form 1120 filing
view.s6655.pen.reasonable_cause: Reasonable cause exception: rarely granted (Boyle case strict)
view.s6655.sched.consolidated: Consolidated group: parent + all subs aggregate
view.s6655.sched.equal: Equal installments OR annualized / seasonal methods
view.s6655.sched.q1: Q1: April 15 — 25% of required annual payment
view.s6655.sched.weekend: If due date is weekend / holiday: next business day
view.s6662.h1.title: // § 6662 ACCURACY-RELATED PENALTY (20%/40%)
view.s6662.hint.intro: 20% on negligence + substantial understatement + valuation misstatement. 40% gross valuation + foreign asset undisclosed. § 6664 reasonable cause defense limited for tax shelters.
view.s6663.badge.consciousness_guilt: 'Consciousness of guilt' behaviors (rescheduling, evading IRS contacts)
view.s6663.badge.evasion: Repeated underpayment + filing failures over multiple years
view.s6663.h1.title: // § 6663 CIVIL FRAUD 75%
view.s6663.hint.intro: 75% of underpayment portion due to fraud. IRS proves by clear + convincing evidence. Once any fraud established, ENTIRE underpayment presumed fraudulent (rebuttable by taxpayer's evidence). No SOL limit (§ 6501(c)(1)). 0.5%/month interest. Civil vs criminal: § 6663 civil; § 7201 criminal (5-yr felony) — government usually wins civil first then indicts.
view.s6663.label.correctly: Correct tax that should have been reported ($)
view.s6663.rel.6651: § 6651 Failure to file: 5%/month up to 25% (or 15%/month if fraudulent)
view.s6663.rel.6694: § 6694 Return preparer: $5k or 75% of preparer's fee for unreasonable position
view.s6664.boyle.body: Reliance on agent for FILING DEADLINE = NOT reasonable cause (Boyle). However, reliance on advice for SUBSTANTIVE TAX positions CAN be reasonable cause if Neonatology factors met. Distinction: 'ministerial / clerical tasks' (taxpayer responsible) vs 'substantive judgment' (reliance OK). 7th Circuit in West v. Comm'r reaffirmed but with nuanced application.
view.s6664.fac.adverse_circumstances: Position taken in adverse-circumstance avoidance pattern
view.s6664.fac.expert_taxpayer: Taxpayer is professional / expert with knowledge
view.s6664.fac.large_amount: Large dollar amount of underpayment relative to total income
view.s6664.fac.late_response: Late response to IRS inquiries / non-cooperation
view.s6664.fac.willful_blindness: Willful blindness — reckless disregard for facts
view.s6664.h1.title: // § 6664 REASONABLE CAUSE DEFENSE
view.s6664.h2.reliance_factors: Valid professional-reliance criteria (Neonatology Associates v. Comm'r)
view.s6664.hint.intro: Statutory defense against § 6662 accuracy + § 6663 civil fraud penalties. Requires (1) reasonable cause + (2) good faith. Burden on TAXPAYER. Factors: effort to assess proper liability, knowledge / experience, reliance on professional advice (with valid-reliance criteria). Doesn't apply to § 6707A reportable transaction penalty (strict liability).
view.s6664.rel.boilerplate: Boilerplate opinions / kit-style memoranda generally don't qualify
view.s6664.rel.discussion: Substantive discussion of facts + applicable law occurred
view.s6664.rel.good_faith_reliance: Taxpayer actually relied in good faith on adviser's judgment
view.s6664.rel.independence: Adviser had no inherent conflict of interest / lack of independence
view.s6664.rel.qualified: Adviser was a competent professional with sufficient expertise
view.s6664.rel.specific_advice: Advice specific to taxpayer's facts (not generic)
view.s6672.h1.title: // § 6672 TRUST FUND RECOVERY PENALTY
view.s6672.hint.intro: 100% personal penalty on responsible person who willfully fails to pay over trust fund taxes (withheld income tax + employee FICA). Joint and several liability + corp veil pierced.
view.s6694.h1.title: // § 6694 PREPARER PENALTY
view.s6694.hint.intro: (a) Unreasonable position: lesser of $1,000 OR 50% of preparer's fee. (b) Willful or reckless: lesser of $5,000 OR 75% of preparer's fee. Reasonable-basis standard: ~33% chance of being sustained. Tax-shelter / reportable transaction: more-likely-than-not standard (>50%). Disclosure (Form 8275 / Form 8275-R) on aggressive position avoids (a). Required Practitioner Registration (PTIN) annually.
view.s6694.rel.6695a: § 6695(a) Failure to furnish copy of return: $60/failure (max $30k)
view.s6694.rel.6695g: § 6695(g) Failure to comply with EITC due diligence: $635/failure (2024)
view.s6694.rel.6700: § 6700 Abusive tax shelter promoter: 50% of gross income from activity
view.s6694.rel.7216: § 7216 Unauthorized disclosure of taxpayer info: $1k + 1-yr prison
view.s6695.c230.fees: § 10.27 fees: no contingent fees on original returns
view.s6695.dd_note: § 6695(g) due diligence FAILURES. Required: Form 8867 each return + retain 3-year records. Penalty PER CREDIT per return — EITC + CTC + AOTC + HoH on same return = up to $635 × 4 = $2,540 per return. IRS preparer compliance unit (CDX) audits high-volume EITC preparers. Implement systematic checklist + documentation procedures.
view.s6695.gg.cant_solely_rely: Cannot rely solely on taxpayer's word — verify documentation
view.s6695.gg.cumulative: EITC + CTC + AOTC + HoH on same return = up to $635 × 4 = $2,540 per return
view.s6695.gg.documentation: Document: SSN cards, dependent records, qualifying expense receipts
view.s6695.gg.form_8867: Form 8867 (Paid Preparer's Due Diligence Checklist) required EACH RETURN
view.s6695.gg.penalty_per_credit: PENALTY PER CREDIT per return — multiple credits → multiple penalties
view.s6695.gg.questions: Required: ask appropriate questions + verify taxpayer eligibility
view.s6695.gg.records: Retain ALL records 3 years from filing date
view.s6695.gg.scope: Refundable credits: EITC, Child Tax Credit (refundable), American Opportunity Tax Credit, Head of Household status
view.s6695.h1.title: // § 6695 PREPARER PENALTIES
view.s6695.h2.s6695g_diligence: § 6695(g) — EITC + CTC + AOTC + HoH due diligence
view.s6695.hint.intro: Various FLAT PENALTIES for procedural failures by tax return preparers. § 6695(a): failure to furnish copy of return to taxpayer — $60 / return. § 6695(b): failure to sign as preparer — $60. § 6695(c): failure to maintain list of returns prepared — $60. § 6695(g): failure to perform due diligence on EITC, CTC, AOTC, HoH — $635 / failure (2024). Form 8867 required for each refundable credit return. Annual penalty caps by failure type. Reasonable cause exception limited.
view.s6695.rc.appeals: Appeals: petition Tax Court within 30 days of denial
view.s6695.rc.documentation: Document reasonable steps taken to verify
view.s6695.rc.factors: Factors: experience, complexity of return, supervisor approval
view.s6695.rc.first_time: First-time penalty: harder to abate (no automatic abatement)
view.s6695.rc.no_reliance: Cannot rely on taxpayer's representations without verification
view.s6695.rc.reliance: Reliance on substantial authority + adequate disclosure
view.s6695.rc.standard: Limited reasonable cause exception (Boyle case strict)
view.s67.h1.title: // § 67 2% MISC ITEMIZED FLOOR + TCJA SUSPENSION
view.s67.hint.intro: Pre-TCJA: 2% AGI floor. § 67(g) TCJA SUSPENDS misc itemized 2018-2025. § 67(b) exceptions (gambling/IRD/impairment) + § 67(e) estate/trust above-line preserved.
view.s6700.h1.title: // § 6700 PROMOTER PENALTY
view.s6700.hint.intro: Penalty on PROMOTERS of abusive tax shelters who: (1) make false/fraudulent statements about tax benefits OR (2) provide GROSS VALUATION OVERSTATEMENTS. Penalty: GREATER of $1,000 per activity OR 100% of gross income derived from the activity. Separate from § 6701 aid + abet ($1,000 individuals; $10,000 corps). Listed Transactions + Reportable Transactions: disclosure requirements + heightened penalties. Forms 8918 + 8886.
view.s6700.listed_note: LISTED TRANSACTION: heightened enforcement. § 6707 $50K failure-to-register + § 6707A $5-200K failure-to-disclose. Material advisor list § 6112 must be maintained + provided to IRS within 20 days. Statute of limitations re-opens 1 yr after disclosure required. DOJ injunction also available (§ 7408).
view.s6700.lt.crispr: CRISPR / R&D credit abuse arrangements — recent
view.s6700.lt.son_of_boss: Son of BOSS / similar — Notice 99-59 (older but active enforcement)
view.s6700.rep.crossborder: FATCA + CRS reporting overlap for offshore arrangements
view.s6700.rep.failure_disclosure: Failure to disclose: $50,000 per failure (more for listed)
view.s6700.rep.form_8886: Form 8886 — Reportable Transaction disclosure by taxpayer
view.s6700.rep.list: Maintained list of advisees (Material Advisor List § 6112)
view.s6700.rep.list_30day: Provide list to IRS within 20 days of request
view.s6700.rep.reportable_transaction_ty: 5 reportable transaction categories: listed, confidential, contractual, loss, transaction of interest
view.s6700.rep.statute_listed: Listed transactions: statute of limitations re-opens for 1 yr after disclosure required
view.s6700.scope.advisor: Material advisor (§ 6111): provides tax-related material aid
view.s6700.scope.compensation_link: Penalty applies to person who derives INCOME from promotion
view.s6700.scope.knowledge_or_should: 'Knew or had reason to know' of false statement
view.s6700.scope.promoter: Promoter: organizes / participates in sale of plan / arrangement
view.s6700.scope.seller: Seller: who sells interests in the arrangement
view.s6700.scope.tax_benefits: 'Tax benefits' includes deductions, credits, exclusions, deferral
view.s6707a.h1.title: // § 6707A REPORTABLE TRANSACTION
view.s6707a.hint.intro: 5 categories: Listed (most aggressive) / Confidential / Loss / Transaction-of-Interest / Contractual Protection. Penalty: 75% of decrease in tax claimed (min $10k / $50k entity, max $100k / $200k entity). Reportable on Form 8886. Strict liability — no reasonable cause defense.
view.s6707a.label.name: Transaction name
view.s6707a.material.body: Material Advisors who promote / draft / sell Reportable Transactions face their own penalties: § 6707: $50k flat (or $200k for Listed) for failing to file Form 8918. § 6708: $10k per day (max $50k) for not maintaining investor / advisee list. Form 13976 required to disclose advisor identity to investors.
view.s6707a.stack.7203: Criminal § 7203 willful failure to file (1-yr misdemeanor)
view.s6707a.th.name: Name
view.s6724.de_minimis.body: 10 returns OR 0.5% of total, whichever is less, with INCORRECT information are PENALTY-FREE if cured by August 1. Excellent reason to do error-correcting Form W-2c / 1099-CORR by July 31. Doesn't apply to 'intentional disregard' failures.
view.s6724.h1.title: // § 6721/22/24 INFO RETURN PENALTIES
view.s6724.hint.intro: § 6721 Failure to file + § 6722 Failure to furnish payee stack. Penalties: $60/$130/$310 per return by tier (cured within 30 days / by Aug 1 / after). $630 intentional. Annual caps: $1.26M small biz / $3.78M large. § 6724 reasonable cause waiver + De Minimis safe harbor (10 or 0.5% of forms cured by Aug 1, whichever less).
view.s6724.rc.acted_quickly: Acted quickly to correct upon discovery
view.s6724.rc.events_beyond_control: Events beyond filer's control (records destroyed, key employee disabled, etc.)
view.s6724.rc.no_warning: No advance IRS warning of the issue
view.s6724.rc.significant_mitigating: Significant mitigating factors despite normal business care
view.s6724.rc.system_software: Software / system failure beyond filer's control
view.s6724.rel.6651: § 6651 Failure to file / pay TAX returns (separate from info returns)
view.s6724.rel.6672: § 6672 Trust Fund Recovery Penalty (TFRP): 100% on unpaid trust fund taxes
view.s6724.rel.6722: § 6722 Failure to furnish CORRECT payee statement to recipient
view.s6724.rel.6723: § 6723 Failure to comply with other info reporting ($60/failure)
view.s6724.rel.electronic: Electronic filing requirement (≥ 10 returns 2024): $310/failure if paper instead
view.s6724.rel.intentional: Intentional disregard penalty: $630/return or 10% of amount required
view.s6724.rel.payee_w9: Failure to obtain W-9 backup withholding $310/failure
view.s691.h1.title: // § 691 IRD INCOME IN RESPECT OF DECEDENT
view.s691.hint.intro: Income earned but not received before death. Beneficiary reports as ORDINARY income when received. NO § 1014 step-up in basis. § 691(c) deduction: recipient takes income tax deduction for estate tax attributable to IRD — prevents double taxation. Most common: traditional IRA / 401(k) balances, accrued interest, accounts receivable, NQ deferred comp, deferred IRC § 453 installment sale.
view.s691.plan.691c: § 691(c) deduction is a MISC itemized — suspended by TCJA through 2025?
view.s691.plan.charity: Name charity as IRA beneficiary — charity avoids income tax
view.s691.plan.crt: Charitable Remainder Trust as IRA beneficiary — stretches income
view.s691.plan.nimcrut: NIMCRUT funded with IRA for stretch-like outcome
view.s691.plan.qpb: 'Eligible designated beneficiary' exceptions: spouse, minor child, disabled, < 10 yr younger
view.s691.plan.roth_convert: Pre-death Roth conversion: removes IRD treatment, gets future-tax-free
view.s691.plan.stretch_secure: SECURE Act 2020 killed lifetime stretch — 10-yr cap for non-spouse
view.s707.h1.title: // § 707 PARTNER-PARTNERSHIP TRANSACTIONS
view.s707.hint.intro: § 707(a)(2)(B) disguised sale 2-yr presumption. § 707(b) related party loss disallowance + gain ordinary. § 707(c) guaranteed payments + SE tax.
view.s7122.ale.food_clothing: Food + clothing + personal care + household supplies + miscellaneous
view.s7122.ale.transport: Transportation: ownership + operating costs by region
view.s7122.h1.title: // § 7122 OFFER IN COMPROMISE
view.s7122.hint.intro: IRS settles for less when full collection doubtful. 3 grounds: (1) Doubt as to Collectibility (DATC), (2) Doubt as to Liability (DATL), (3) Effective Tax Administration (ETA). Reasonable Collection Potential (RCP) = net equity + future income × 12 mo (lump) or 24 mo (periodic). $205 application fee + Form 656 + Form 433-A(OIC) / 433-B(OIC). Low-income waiver available.
view.s7122.po.application_fee: $205 application fee (waived if low-income or DATL)
view.s7122.po.default: Default: full balance owed + add'l penalties
view.s7122.po.deferred: Deferred Payment Plan: pay over remaining CSED (rare, after IA failure)
view.s7122.po.during_eval: Payments during eval keep accruing — non-refundable if rejected
view.s7122.po.lump_sum: Lump sum: 20% with offer + 5 payments within 5 months of acceptance
view.s7122.po.periodic: Periodic: initial + monthly payments DURING evaluation + after acceptance, 24 mo total
view.s72p.ben.flexible: Can be re-paid early without penalty
view.s72p.ben.interest_to_you: Interest goes BACK to your account (paying yourself)
view.s72p.ben.low_rate: Interest typically lower than credit cards / personal loans
view.s72p.ben.no_credit: No credit check (loan from your own balance)
view.s72p.ben.no_tax: Not a taxable distribution if repaid on time
view.s72p.h1.title: // § 72(p) 401(k) PLAN LOAN
view.s72p.hint.intro: Max: lesser of $50,000 OR 50% vested balance. 5-year max term (up to 30 yrs for principal residence). Quarterly payments minimum. Loan default = deemed distribution + 10% penalty if < 59½. SECURE 2.0: Roth source for loans + plan-to-plan rollover loans preserved. Hidden cost: opportunity cost (money out of market) + double taxation of interest (you repay with after-tax dollars, then pay tax again on withdrawal).
view.s72p.risk.balloon: Balloon payment at term-end if quarterly amounts insufficient
view.s72p.risk.contributions: Suspend new contributions during loan period (some plans)
view.s72p.risk.double_tax: Double-taxation: interest paid with after-tax dollars + taxed again on withdrawal
view.s72p.risk.job_loss: Job loss / separation triggers loan acceleration (60-90 days to repay)
view.s72p.risk.market_timing: Borrowing in down market locks in losses
view.s72p.risk.opportunity: Opportunity cost: money OUT of market while loan outstanding
view.s72t.h1.title: // § 72(t) SEPP CALCULATOR
view.s72t.hint.intro: Early IRA / 401(k) withdrawal before 59½ without 10% penalty via Substantially Equal Periodic Payments. THREE approved methods: RMD, Amortization, Annuitization. Must continue ≥ 5 years OR until 59½ (whichever later). MODIFY in those years and 10% penalty applies back to year 1 + interest. SECURE 2.0 floors rate at max(5%, 120% AFR).
view.s72t.mod.account_zero: Account goes to zero (no longer 'substantially equal')
view.s72t.mod.amount_change: Changing the amount (except amort→RMD one-time switch)
view.s72t.mod.rollover: Rolling money IN OR OUT of the SEPP account
view.s72t.note.amort: Balance amortized at rate over life expectancy
view.s731.h1.title: // § 731 PARTNERSHIP DISTRIBUTIONS
view.s731.hint.intro: Gain if cash distributed > outside basis. Loss only on liquidating distribution of cash + receivables + inventory. § 731(c) marketable securities treated as money.
view.s7345.excl.identity: Identified by IRS as identity theft victim
view.s7345.h1.title: // § 7345 PASSPORT REVOCATION
view.s7345.hint.intro: 'Seriously delinquent tax debt' = > $62,000 (2024, indexed) assessed federal tax + lien filed OR levy issued. IRS certifies to State Dept; passport revoked / denied. CP508C notice issued; right to reverse via payment / installment agreement / CDP hearing. Issued passports may be LIMITED to direct US return. Foreign / US-Mexican border crossings denied.
view.s7345.rem.cdp_request: Request CDP hearing (Form 12153 within 30 days of notice)
view.s7345.rem.court: § 7345(e) suit in district court / Tax Court (limited remedies)
view.s7345.rem.emergency: Emergency passport for direct US return: contact embassy/consulate
view.s7345.rem.expedite: Imminent international travel: contact Taxpayer Advocate for expedited reversal
view.s7345.rem.pay_full: Pay in full — IRS reverses within 30 days
view.s7345.warning.act_now: ACT NOW: enter installment agreement / OIC / CDP hearing BEFORE IRS certifies to State Dept. Once certified (CP508C), passport revocation can be immediate on next application / renewal. 30-day reversal after qualifying action.
view.s736.h1.title: // § 736 RETIRING / DECEASED PARTNER PAYMENTS
view.s736.hint.intro: § 736(b) capital for partnership property. § 736(a) ordinary (distributive share or guaranteed payment). § 736(b)(2) service PS exception for unrealized receivables + unstated goodwill.
view.s743.h1.title: // § 743 PARTNERSHIP BASIS ADJUSTMENT
view.s743.hint.intro: § 743(b) aligns transferee inside basis with outside basis when § 754 election OR substantial built-in loss > $250K. § 755 allocates between property classes.
view.s7430.costs.admin: Administrative costs from time IRS could have settled
view.s7430.costs.attorney: Attorney fees at lesser of actual rate or $230/hr (2024)
view.s7430.costs.copying: Court costs + filing fees + copying / postage
view.s7430.costs.no_punitive: NO punitive damages — actual costs only
view.s7430.costs.special: Special factor enhancement: $230/hr can be exceeded for unusual specialization
view.s7430.costs.travel: Reasonable travel costs for taxpayer + counsel
view.s7430.h1.title: // § 7430 ATTORNEY FEES RECOVERY
view.s7430.hint.intro: Prevailing taxpayer can recover reasonable admin + litigation costs from gov't. Conditions: (1) substantially prevailed on amount or most significant issue, (2) net worth < $2M individual / $7M corp / 500 employees, (3) IRS position NOT substantially justified. Cap: $230/hr (2024, indexed). Qualified offer: recover even if didn't 'prevail' if IRS later got worse outcome.
view.s7430.qo.body: Taxpayer makes settlement offer in writing during admin process. If IRS rejects + later obtains a LESS favorable outcome (taxpayer wins more), § 7430 entitles taxpayer to costs from date of offer onwards REGARDLESS of substantially-justified question. Strong tool to flush out marginal cases + force IRS settlement.
view.s7430.sj.amorphous: Standard is amorphous; success depends heavily on facts
view.s7430.sj.factual_dispute: Factual disputes generally favor gov't if reasonable inferences possible
view.s7430.sj.law_change: Subsequent change in law doesn't make prior position unjustified
view.s7430.sj.objective: Objective test from gov't perspective at time of position
view.s7430.sj.pierce: Pierce v. Underwood: 'reasonable basis in both law + fact'
view.s7430.sj.split_decisions: Cases where appellate courts split favor gov't (no clearly settled rule)
view.s7430.sj.taxpayer_burden: Burden ON taxpayer to prove NOT substantially justified
view.s7491.c.basic: IRS bears burden of production on penalties / additions to tax
view.s7491.c.cure_window: Reasonable cause: taxpayer + facts; IRS gets to rebut
view.s7491.c.fraud_penalty: § 6663 fraud: IRS has burden of PROOF (not just production)
view.s7491.c.graev_chai: Graev v. Comm'r (2017) + Chai v. Comm'r (2017): super approval = invalidating defective penalties
view.s7491.c.individual: Individual: applies when penalty ≥ $1M income year
view.s7491.c.s6651: § 6651 failure to file / pay: IRS production only
view.s7491.c.s6664: § 6664(c) reasonable cause exception: taxpayer still bears burden on cure
view.s7491.c.s7491c_2: § 7491(c)(2): supervisor approval requirement for penalty assertion
view.s7491.exc.cfc: Controlled Foreign Corp / subpart F issues
view.s7491.exc.civil_fraud: § 6663 civil fraud (75% penalty) — IRS always has burden
view.s7491.exc.injunction: Injunction proceedings against IRS — different standards
view.s7491.exc.refund_suit: Refund suit (district court / claims court) — burden on plaintiff
view.s7491.exc.summary: Summary judgment: burden under FRCP 56 standards
view.s7491.fees.prevailing: Taxpayer must PREVAIL on substantive issues
view.s7491.fees.purpose: Recover attorney's fees if IRS position NOT SUBSTANTIALLY JUSTIFIED
view.s7491.fees.section_6402: Coordinate with § 6402 set-off + refund offset rules
view.s7491.fees.tax_court: Tax Court awards fees only after final disposition
view.s7491.h1.title: // § 7491 BURDEN OF PROOF
view.s7491.hint.intro: IRS Restructuring + Reform Act 1998: shifted burden to IRS in certain cases. Burden shifts if: (1) taxpayer satisfies RECORD-KEEPING, (2) COOPERATES with IRS, (3) provides reasonable case to IRS administratively. EXCEPTIONS: criminal cases, foreign-source income, partnership audit issues, controlled foreign corp issues. § 7491(c): IRS bears burden on PENALTY for $1M+ individual income (and certain dollar-defined penalties). § 7491(a)(2)(A): individual + net worth requirements for full shift.
view.s7491.req.administrative: ADMINISTRATIVE: provided reasonable case to IRS BEFORE Tax Court (not just first there)
view.s7491.req.cooperation: COOPERATION: with reasonable IRS information requests + substantiation
view.s7491.req.cooperative_meaning: 'Cooperative' = same as discovery + reasonable response time
view.s7491.req.factual_issue: FACTUAL ISSUE: applies to factual issues, not legal questions
view.s7491.req.individual_corp: INDIVIDUAL or CORP: limited to individuals + small corps (≤ $7M net worth + ≤ 500 employees)
view.s7491.req.records: RECORD-KEEPING: produced contemporaneous records adequate to substantiate position
view.s7491.shifted_note: BURDEN SHIFTED to IRS. Strong tactical advantage in Tax Court. Affidavits + business records become powerful evidence. IRS examiner now must prove deficiency rather than taxpayer disproving it. Combine with § 7430 attorney's fees recovery if IRS position not substantially justified.
view.s7508a.combat.body: Service in combat zone: 180-day extension + period of service + period in hospital. Spouses of military in combat zone: same extension. No interest charged. Direct contact: Form 9000 / IRS Combat Zone Hotline. Spouse + dependents file under combatant's protection.
view.s7508a.h1.title: // § 7508A DISASTER POSTPONEMENT
view.s7508a.hint.intro: IRS announces postponement after FEMA disaster declaration. Up to 1 year. Postpones filing, payment, refund claim deadlines. Also COVID-19 (§ 7508A(d) 2020-2021). Combat zones (§ 7508) separate provision. Automatic for taxpayers in affected county — no action required, but put disaster name on top of return. Late filing penalties + interest NOT charged during postponement.
view.s7508a.what.casualty_relief: § 165(i) election to claim casualty loss in prior year
view.s7508a.what.no_interest: No interest accrues during postponement on tax owed
view.s752.h1.title: // § 752 PARTNERSHIP LIABILITIES + BASIS
view.s752.hint.intro: § 752(a) liability increase = deemed contribution + § 752(b) decrease = deemed distribution. Recourse via constructive liquidation. Nonrecourse via 3-tier allocation.
view.s754.alloc.classes: Split partnership assets into 'capital gain' and 'ordinary income' classes
view.s754.alloc.depreciable: Adjustment recovered over remaining life of asset
view.s754.alloc.no_increase: No increase in basis of partnership cash / receivables (already at FMV)
view.s754.alloc.ordinary_first: Ordinary class allocated first to extent of unrealized OI; remainder to capital class
view.s754.alloc.real_estate: Real estate: stepped-up basis becomes new depreciation schedule for buyer
view.s754.alloc.within_class: Within class: by relative FMV of assets
view.s754.h1.title: // § 754 PARTNERSHIP STEP-UP
view.s754.hint.intro: § 743(b) adjustment on TRANSFER of partnership interest (sale, death). Steps up (or down) the buyer's INSIDE basis in partnership assets to match OUTSIDE basis paid. § 734(b) adjustment on partnership DISTRIBUTION when distributee's outside basis differs from inside basis of distributed property. § 754 election = one-time irrevocable (rev. w/ IRS consent). Mandatory § 743(b) if substantial built-in loss (> $250K). Partnership-level election affects ALL transfers in / out.
view.s754.trade.con_admin: CON: administrative burden (track adjustment per partner per asset)
view.s754.trade.con_step_down: CON: mandatory step-down on loss transfers; cannot opt out post-2017
view.s754.trade.future_transfers: Affects FUTURE transfers as well — both sales + deaths thereafter
view.s754.trade.permanent: PERMANENT: irrevocable except with IRS consent
view.s754.trade.pro_step_up: PRO: avoid phantom income for buyer; align inside / outside basis after transfers
view.s754.trade.ptp: Publicly traded partnership: § 754 elections nearly always in effect
view.s754.trade.real_estate: Real estate partnerships: § 754 nearly universal because step-up offsets depreciation recapture
view.s7701b.cc.183_in_year: NOT available if 183+ days in current year alone
view.s7701b.cc.dual_residence: Dual residence: invoke treaty tie-breaker article instead
view.s7701b.cc.factors: Factors: family, home, personal belongings, social ties, voting
view.s7701b.cc.no_green: CANNOT apply if Green Card applied for or pending
view.s7701b.cc.spt_met: Available when SPT met but tax home is foreign + closer connection
view.s7701b.cc.tax_home_foreign: Tax home: principal place of business / employment
view.s7701b.cc.timely: Must file Form 8840 timely with Form 1040NR
view.s7701b.exc.athlete: Professional athlete competing in charitable sports event
view.s7701b.exc.dual_status: Dual-status year: residence may start / end mid-year
view.s7701b.exc.student: Student on F, J, M visa (limited to 5 calendar years)
view.s7701b.exc.teacher_trainee: Teacher / trainee on J or Q visa (limited to 2 of past 6 yrs)
view.s7701b.h1.title: // § 7701(b) RESIDENT ALIEN
view.s7701b.hint.intro: US RESIDENT ALIEN if: (1) Green Card test (LPR status) OR (2) Substantial Presence Test (SPT). SPT: 31 days current year + 183 days weighted (current + 1/3 prior + 1/6 two prior). Closer connection exception (Form 8840): SPT met but closer to foreign country. Treaty tie-breaker: dual residents — treaty residence rules apply. Resident alien: taxed on WORLDWIDE income. Nonresident alien: US-source only.
view.s7701b.label.exempt: Exempt individual (J, F, M, Q visa)?
view.s7701b.nonresident_note: NONRESIDENT ALIEN: taxed only on US-source income + ECI. File Form 1040NR. 30% FDAP withholding on US-source passive income. Capital gains on non-real-estate generally exempt. § 871(m) dividend equivalent payments separately analyzed.
view.s7701b.resident_note: RESIDENT ALIEN: subject to US tax on WORLDWIDE income. File Form 1040. May elect treaty tie-breaker via Form 8833 if dual resident under treaty. Must comply with FBAR, Form 8938 if foreign assets > $10K / $50K thresholds. § 911 FEIE may apply if working abroad.
view.s7701b.spt.183: Step 3: If weighted total ≥ 183 → RESIDENT ALIEN
view.s7701b.spt.31_min: Step 1: Count current-year days — must be ≥ 31 (else NOT resident regardless of formula)
view.s7701b.spt.commuter: Daily commuters from Canada / Mexico: NOT counted
view.s7701b.spt.day_count: Day count: any day physically in US (even partial day = full day)
view.s7701b.spt.exempt_days: Exempt individual (F, J, M, Q visa) days don't count
view.s7701b.spt.medical: Medical condition keep days don't count if traveled w/ intent to leave
view.s7701b.spt.transit: Transit through US (< 24 hrs): NOT counted
view.s7701b.spt.weighted: Step 2: Weighted formula: current + 1/3 prior + 1/6 two prior
view.s7701b.tt.center_vital: Test 2: Center of vital interests — closer connections
view.s7701b.tt.disclosure_8833: Must file Form 8833 to invoke treaty position
view.s7701b.tt.dual_resident: Used when person is resident under BOTH US and foreign country domestic law
view.s7701b.tt.mutual_agreement: Test 5: Mutual agreement between competent authorities
view.s7701b.tt.permanent_home: Test 1: Permanent home — country with permanent home wins
view.s7701b.tt.us_treaty_residency: Treaty residency: tax as nonresident in non-residence country
view.s7701o.case.castle_harbor: Castle Harbor (CA-3 2009): partnership tax shelter rebuffed
view.s7701o.case.coltec: Coltec Industries (Fed. Cir. 2006): two-prong test articulated
view.s7701o.case.compaq_castle: Compaq v. Comm'r (5th Cir. 2002): foreign tax credit shelter struck
view.s7701o.case.frank_lyon: Frank Lyon (1978): sale-leaseback substance over form
view.s7701o.case.gregory: Gregory v. Helvering (1935): foundation case — 'business purpose' required
view.s7701o.case.salem_financial: Salem Financial (2014): UK / US dividend stripping shelter
view.s7701o.case.son_of_boss: 'Son of BOSS' shelters: 2000s wave struck down by economic substance doctrine
view.s7701o.fail_note: Transaction FAILS economic substance: TAX BENEFITS DISALLOWED + 20-40% STRICT LIABILITY penalty (no reasonable cause defense). Disclosure (Form 8275) reduces penalty 40% → 20%. Verify BOTH prongs PRE-transaction with qualified tax opinion + contemporaneous documentation of business purpose.
view.s7701o.h1.title: // § 7701(o) ECONOMIC SUBSTANCE
view.s7701o.hint.intro: Economic substance doctrine CODIFIED 2010 (Health Care Act). Transaction has economic substance only if BOTH: (1) meaningful change in economic position + (2) substantial non-tax purpose. CONJUNCTIVE — both required. Violations: § 6662(b)(6) 20% accuracy penalty (40% if undisclosed); STRICT LIABILITY (no reasonable cause defense). Targets: tax shelters, listed transactions, abusive transactions. Pre-2010: common law 'two-prong' was disjunctive in some circuits, conjunctive in others.
view.s7701o.pen.20pct: 20% on understatement attributable to economic substance violation
view.s7701o.pen.contrast: Contrast: regular § 6662 penalty has reasonable cause defense
view.s7701o.pen.disclosure: Disclosure via Form 8275: lowers penalty from 40% to 20%
view.s7701o.pen.no_settlement: No settlement / amnesty programs for § 7701(o) violations historically
view.s7701o.pen.qualified_opinion: Qualified opinion (more-likely-than-not) does NOT avoid § 7701(o) penalty
view.s7701o.pen.return_preparer: Return preparer penalty § 6694 applies for 'unreasonable position'
view.s7701o.pen.strict: STRICT LIABILITY: § 6664(c)(2) — no 'reasonable cause' defense for § 7701(o)
view.s7701o.t.aggregate_or_separate: Aggregate or separately analyze — Commissioner discretion
view.s7701o.t.both: BOTH required — pre-2010 some circuits used disjunctive (either / or)
view.s7701o.t.financial_accounting: Financial accounting purpose alone DOES NOT count as substantial non-tax
view.s7701o.t.objective: OBJECTIVE prong: meaningful change in economic position — pre-tax profit potential
view.s7701o.t.profit_substantial: Pre-tax profit must be SUBSTANTIAL relative to TAX BENEFIT
view.s7701o.t.relevant: Doctrine RELEVANT to transaction (not all transactions)
view.s7701o.t.subjective: SUBJECTIVE prong: substantial non-tax purpose (business / personal motive)
view.s7701o.t.taxpayer_burden: Taxpayer burden of proof on both prongs
view.s7702.c.effect: Effect: ALLOWS HIGHER CASH VALUE relative to death benefit
