view.s7702.c.future_review: Future: rates re-evaluated periodically based on AFR (no Congressional action)
view.s7702.c.industry: Industry impact: more flexible accumulation products available
view.s7702.c.lower_floor: Interest rate floor lowered from 4% to 2% (60% of long-term AFR)
view.s7702.c.products: Products developed: high cash value whole life designed under new rules
view.s7702.c.transition: Transition: existing policies grandfathered; new policies use new rules
view.s7702.cvat.advantage: CVAT advantage: more cash value buildup permitted (less restrictive)
view.s7702.cvat.common: Common for: whole life, single premium policies
view.s7702.cvat.disadvantage: CVAT disadvantage: must constantly increase death benefit or contract becomes non-qualifying
view.s7702.cvat.formula: Net single premium based on minimum mortality (CSO tables) + minimum interest rate
view.s7702.cvat.mortality: Mortality: 2001 Commissioners' Standard Ordinary (CSO) — replaced 1980 CSO
view.s7702.cvat.principle: Cash value at any time MAY NOT exceed net single premium for death benefit at that point
view.s7702.cvat.simplification: Calculation: per $1,000 of death benefit × insured's age (CSO table)
view.s7702.fail.charitable: Charitable: contribution of policy may be subject to § 170 limitations
view.s7702.fail.cure: Cure: adjust death benefit OR refund excess premiums
view.s7702.fail.investor_owned_life: Investor-owned life insurance (IOLI): § 264 disallows interest deduction
view.s7702.fail.mec: MEC § 7702A: distributions taxed FIFO income-first + 10% penalty < age 59.5
view.s7702.fail.no_death_benefit: Death benefit may still be tax-free under § 101
view.s7702.fail.no_loans_tax_free: Loans against cash value: now taxable (instead of tax-free)
view.s7702.fail.taxable: Failure: cash value increase taxed as ORDINARY INCOME each year
view.s7702.fail_note: POLICY FAILED § 7702. Cash value buildup TAXED as ordinary income each year. Loans against cash value: TAXABLE. Death benefit may still be tax-free under § 101(a). CURE: increase death benefit (raise corridor) OR refund excess premiums + interest. Insurance company should self-monitor + warn before failure. Consult tax + insurance counsel before policy redesign.
view.s7702.gpt.advantage: GPT advantage: lower required death benefit relative to cash value
view.s7702.gpt.common: Common for: universal life, variable life
view.s7702.gpt.corridor_ages: Corridor %: 250% at age 40, declines to 100% at age 95
view.s7702.gpt.cvct: PLUS Cash Value Corridor Test: cash value ≤ % of death benefit
view.s7702.gpt.disadvantage: GPT disadvantage: less premium flexibility / lower cash accumulation
view.s7702.gpt.level: Guideline level premium: amortized over insured's lifetime
view.s7702.gpt.single: Guideline single premium: actuarial computation based on CSO + interest rate
view.s7702.gpt.test: Sum of premiums paid cannot exceed guideline single + guideline level premium limits
view.s7702.h1.title: // § 7702 LIFE INSURANCE CONTRACT
view.s7702.hint.intro: Defines what qualifies as life insurance for tax purposes (cash value buildup tax-deferred). Must satisfy: (1) CVAT (Cash Value Accumulation Test) OR (2) GPT (Guideline Premium Test) + Cash Value Corridor. Failure: cash value taxed as ordinary income each year (tax-deferred status lost). § 7702A — MEC (Modified Endowment Contract): separate test prevents abuse via large premiums. 2021 update: interest rate floor lowered from 4% to 2% (Consolidated Appropriations Act 2021).
view.s7702a.ben.fifo: Cash withdrawals: FIFO (basis first), then gains
view.s7702a.ben.loans: Policy loans NOT taxable income (treated as borrowed)
view.s7702a.h1.title: // § 7702A MEC 7-PAY TEST
view.s7702a.hint.intro: Life insurance contract failing the 7-pay test becomes a Modified Endowment Contract (MEC). MEC distributions / loans treated as TAXABLE INCOME first (LIFO) + 10% penalty if < 59½. Cum premiums > sum of 7 net level premiums in first 7 yrs → MEC. Material change (raise death benefit, certain option swaps) restarts 7-year window. Death benefit + cash-value-loan-payoff is tax-free for non-MEC contracts.
view.s7702a.label.basis: Basis in contract (cum premiums - prior dist) ($)
view.s7702a.pen.10pct: 10% penalty under 59½ unless death / disability / SEPP
view.s7702a.pen.collateral: Pledge as loan collateral = deemed distribution
view.s7702a.pen.lifo: Distributions / loans treated as INCOME FIRST (LIFO)
view.s7702a.pen.ord_income: Income portion taxed as ORDINARY (not LTCG)
view.s7702a.use.elderly: Elderly individuals using MEC as legacy vehicle
view.s7702a.use.heirs: Plan to leave death benefit to heirs untaxed
view.s7702a.use.high_premium: Maximize tax-deferred growth, pay all premiums upfront
view.s7811.h1.title: // § 7811 TAXPAYER ASSISTANCE ORDER
view.s7811.hint.intro: Taxpayer Advocate Service (TAS) issues TAO ordering IRS action when 'significant hardship' exists. 4 criteria: (1) immediate threat of adverse action, (2) delay > 30 days, (3) cost (including professional fees), (4) irreparable injury / long-term adverse impact. Free service. Form 911 or 1-877-777-4778. TAS can override IRS Operations Manual.
view.s7811.note.file_now: File Form 911 (Application for Taxpayer Assistance Order) or call 1-877-777-4778. Free service. Acknowledgment within 1 business day; case assigned within 5 days. Bring all IRS correspondence + documentation of hardship.
view.s7811.tao.appeal: IRS can appeal to TAS Deputy Chief; TAO stays in effect during appeal
view.s7811.tao.can_recommend: Cannot recommend tax position; only procedural relief
view.s7811.tao.compel: Compels IRS to act (release levy, issue refund, etc.) within specified time
view.s7811.tao.no_court: No automatic court enforcement (TAS is administrative)
view.s7811.tao.override: Can override IRM but NOT statutory mandates
view.s7811.tao.relief_types: Common relief: levy release within 24 hrs in true emergencies
view.s7811.tao.timeline: Acknowledgment within 1 business day; case manager within 5 days
view.s7811.tas.fix_processing: Fix processing errors + restore correct records
view.s7811.tas.legal_advice: NOT legal advice or tax planning
view.s7811.tas.research_change: Research + policy change recommendations via Annual Report to Congress
view.s7811.tas.systemic: Systemic Advocacy: address recurring IRS process failures
view.s7872.afr.bracket: Short = ≤ 3 yr, mid = > 3 ≤ 9 yr, long = > 9 yr
view.s7872.afr.fred: Bloomberg, FRED, IRS website all carry monthly AFR tables
view.s7872.afr.irs: IRS publishes monthly via Rev. Rul. (typically third Friday of prior month)
view.s7872.afr.month_lock: Use rate from month loan is made (or lower of two prior months for term loan)
view.s7872.h1.title: // § 7872 INTRA-FAMILY LOAN / AFR
view.s7872.hint.intro: Loan to family below the Applicable Federal Rate (AFR) → IRS imputes interest income to lender + may impute a gift. AFR is the IRS-published minimum monthly: short-term (≤3 yr), mid-term (3-9 yr), long-term (>9 yr). $10,000 gift loan safe harbor entirely exempt. $100,000 limit: imputed income capped at borrower's net investment income.
view.s7874.h1.title: // § 7874 ANTI-INVERSION
view.s7874.hint.intro: Foreign acquirer of US corp treated as US corp ('surrogate foreign corporation') if former US shareholders own 80%+. 60-79%: stays foreign but loses tax benefits ('inversion gain' taxed at 35% — § 4985 also taxes officers + directors 20% excise). SBA exception: 25% employees + assets + income in foreign jurisdiction. 2016 regs: 3-year lookback on serial inversions.
view.s7874.hist.modern: Modern inversions rare due to combined § 245A repatriation + GILTI minimum
view.s7874.hist.notice2014_52: Notice 2014-52 attacked cash-box mergers + stuffing transactions
view.s7874.hist.regs2016: 2016 final + temp regs: 3-year lookback on serial inversions, killed Pfizer-Allergan
view.s7874.label.us_pct: Former US shareholder % of foreign acquirer
view.s7874.sba.all_three: ALL THREE prongs required — not 2 of 3
view.s7874.sba.bermuda_caps: Bermuda + Caymans: SBA almost impossible to satisfy due to no real economy
view.s7874.sba.ireland_potential: Ireland + Netherlands: real ops possible due to economic substance
view.s7874.sba.real_business: Real operating business, not shell company
view.s7874.sba.tax_residency: Tax residency in test country (not flag of convenience)
view.s7874.surrogate_note: Foreign acquirer treated as US corp for ALL purposes — worldwide income subject to US tax, no treaty benefits, dividends from foreign subs taxed without § 245A. Anti-inversion penalty.
view.s79.disc.actual_cost: Actual cost = GREATER of actual premium OR Table I uniform cost
view.s79.disc.consequence: Discriminatory plan → KEY EMPLOYEES taxable on FULL coverage cost (no $50K excl)
view.s79.disc.cure: Cure: equalize coverage / benefits or carve out keys to separate plan
view.s79.disc.test_b: Benefits test: cannot favor key employees in benefits
view.s79.disc.test_e: Eligibility test: cannot favor key employees in coverage
view.s79.disc_note: Discriminatory plan + key employee: lose $50K exclusion → FULL coverage cost taxable (GREATER of actual premium or Table I uniform cost). Cure: carve out keys to separate plan OR equalize coverage / benefits across all employees.
view.s79.h1.title: // § 79 GROUP TERM LIFE
view.s79.h2.uniform: Table I — Uniform Premiums per $1K per month (Notice 2021-65)
view.s79.hint.intro: First $50,000 of employer-provided group term life coverage = TAX-FREE. Coverage over $50K: IMPUTED INCOME based on IRS Uniform Premium Table I (Notice 2021-65). Formula: (Coverage − $50K) / 1,000 × IRS rate per $1K × months covered. Reported W-2 Box 12 code C. Subject to Soc Sec + Medicare (but NOT income tax withholding). Discrimination rules: if plan favors key employees, full coverage taxable to them (no $50K exclusion).
view.s79.spec.beneficiary: Beneficiary received death benefit: tax-free under § 101(a)
view.s79.spec.cafeteria: § 125 election to pay premiums pre-tax: reduces imputed income
view.s79.spec.carry_over: Coverage continuation: months past employment count
view.s79.spec.dependent: Dependent coverage ≤ $2,000: de minimis — no income
view.s79.spec.disabled: Disabled employees: tax-free up to limit
view.s79.spec.permanent: Permanent benefits (cash value): full value taxable in year of acquisition
view.s79.spec.retired: Retired employees: continued coverage may still be § 79 subject
view.s79.spec.spousal: Spousal coverage ≤ $2,000: de minimis — no income
view.s83.adv.amt_avoid: ISO context: avoid AMT preference item on exercise (combined w/ § 422)
view.s83.adv.cap_gain: Starts capital gain holding period → LTCG (20%/15%/0%) on appreciation
view.s83.adv.estate_planning: Estate planning: lock in low basis NOW for gift / transfer at low value
view.s83.adv.low_fmv: Lock in LOW grant FMV → ordinary income on $0-ish at grant (founder stock)
view.s83.adv.partnership_capital: Profits interest: receive partnership interest, no ordinary income (Rev. Proc. 93-27 safe harbor)
view.s83.adv.public_company: Public company: low-vol stock = small risk; high-vol = significant risk if forfeit
view.s83.adv.shelter: Shelter all future appreciation as LTCG instead of ordinary on vest
view.s83.forfeit_note: Forfeited after § 83(b) election: ordinary income still recognized at grant. NO REFUND of tax paid. Only relief: capital loss equal to PRICE PAID (NOT tax basis) — limited to $3K/yr against ordinary. This is the major risk of § 83(b) — confirm vesting probability before electing.
view.s83.h1.title: // § 83 RESTRICTED PROPERTY
view.s83.hint.intro: Property received for services taxed at FMV WHEN VESTED (substantial risk of forfeiture lapses). § 83(b) election: tax at GRANT — locks in low FMV; starts capital gain clock. 30-day deadline; irrevocable. If forfeit BEFORE vest: no deduction for tax paid (cap loss only if § 83(b)). Common: founder stock, restricted stock awards (RSAs), profits interests (Rev. Proc. 93-27). RSUs: NOT § 83 (separate § 451 timing).
view.s83.mech.2015_reg_change: 2015 reg: no longer required to attach to current-year return — but recommended
view.s83.mech.30_days: Within 30 days of GRANT (not vest) — postmark counts
view.s83.mech.address: IRS service center where you file 1040 (find at irs.gov)
view.s83.mech.cash_payment_option: Option: have employer withhold via additional W-2 wages + tax withholding
view.s83.mech.copy_to_employer: Copy to: employer (W-2 reporting) + retain copy + attach to current-year return
view.s83.mech.no_email: EMAIL only is NOT sufficient — must be physical mail (certified)
view.s83.mech.payment: Pay tax at marginal rate × (FMV − price paid) × shares
view.s83.mech.statement: File: written statement (IRS sample) — name, SSN, property description, grant date, FMV, restrictions
view.s83.risk.30_days: 30-DAY deadline: certified mail + return receipt + email copy + accountant
view.s83.risk.appraisal: 409A appraisal: PRE-FUNDING risk if FMV not defensible
view.s83.risk.capital_loss: Forfeiture → only CAPITAL LOSS (limited $3K/yr against ordinary)
view.s83.risk.cash_flow: Cash flow: need cash to pay tax on illiquid stock
view.s83.risk.high_fmv: High FMV at grant: tax bill may exceed value
view.s83.risk.late_election: Late election: NO RELIEF except mistake / duress; signed waiver insufficient
view.s83.risk.no_refund: Forfeiture → NO refund of tax paid (sunk cost)
view.s83.risk.tax_now: Tax PAID NOW even if stock never vests
view.s86.f.first_tier: First tier: PI > first threshold → 50% of excess (up to 50% of SS) taxable
view.s86.f.foreign: Foreign-earned income excluded under § 911 still counts in AGI for § 86
view.s86.f.mfs: MFS living with spouse: $0 thresholds → 85% always taxable
view.s86.f.provisional: Provisional Income = AGI + tax-exempt interest + 50% of SS benefits
view.s86.f.second_tier: Second tier: PI > second threshold → 85% of excess + first tier amount; capped at 85% of SS
view.s86.h1.title: // § 86 SOCIAL SECURITY TAXABILITY
view.s86.hint.intro: 0%, 50%, or 85% of SS benefits taxable based on Provisional Income. Provisional Income = AGI + tax-exempt interest + 50% of SS benefits. Thresholds: $25k / $34k (single), $32k / $44k (MFJ). NOT indexed to inflation since 1983/1993 — highly criticized. Tier 1 RRSI treated identically; Tier 2 Railroad retirement taxed differently (more like pension).
view.s86.p.couple_strategy: Married couples: file MFJ (much higher thresholds vs MFS $0)
view.s86.p.delay: Delay SS to 70 (DRC 8% each year) — taxable but at higher amount
view.s86.p.muni_no_help: Muni bond interest INCLUDED in provisional income — doesn't help § 86
view.s86.p.no_indexing: No inflation indexing: 50 million seniors paying tax they didn't expect (1983 affected 10%, 2024 affects ~55%)
view.s86.p.qcd: QCD ($105k 2024) reduces RMD inclusion in AGI
view.s86.p.realized_cap_gains: Manage realized capital gains around § 86 thresholds
view.s86.p.roth_conversion: Roth conversions in low-income years before SS starts
view.s86.p.spend_down_tax_deferred: Spend tax-deferred accounts before SS starts (lower AGI when SS adds in)
view.s86.p.standard_deduction: Standard deduction doesn't reduce SS taxability (already below the line)
view.s86.state.changes: Recent trend: states phasing out SS taxability (NE + UT 2024)
view.s86.state.exempt: Specifically exempt SS: AL, AZ, AR, CA, DE, GA, HI, ID, IL, IN, IA (post-2023), KY, LA, ME, MD, MA, MI, MS, NV (no tax), NJ, NY, NC, OH, OK, OR, PA, SC, VA, WI
view.s86.state.full: Full tax: federal only as of 2024 in remaining states
view.s86.state.no_tax: No state income tax states: WA, TX, FL, NV, AK, SD, WY, TN, NH
view.s86.state.partial: Partial tax: CO, CT, KS, MN, MO (phased out), MT, NE (phased out), NM, RI, UT, VT, WV
view.s871.h1.title: // § 871 NONRESIDENT ALIEN TAX
view.s871.hint.intro: 30% FDAP withholding on US-source passive + graduated ECI on US TB income. § 871(m) dividend equivalents on equity derivatives. Portfolio interest exempt.
view.s871a.exem.bank_dep: § 871(i) bank deposit interest: US bank deposits to foreign payee (since 2013)
view.s871a.exem.cap_gains: Capital gains: NRAs generally NOT taxed except (1) US real prop (§ 897 FIRPTA), (2) ECI, (3) 183-day rule for individuals
view.s871a.exem.casino: Certain casino gambling: blackjack, baccarat, craps, roulette, big-6 wheel
view.s871a.exem.original_issue_short: Original Issue Discount on short-term obligations (≤ 183 days)
view.s871a.exem.portfolio: § 871(h) portfolio interest: registered debt + foreign payee + non-10% shareholder of issuer
view.s871a.fdap.compensation: Compensation for personal services performed in US
view.s871a.fdap.gambling: Gambling winnings (mostly 30% — exempt: blackjack, baccarat, craps, roulette, big-6 in casinos)
view.s871a.fdap.original_issue: OID accruals + capital gain dividends from RIC / REIT
view.s871a.fdap.rent_personal: Rent from personal property in US
view.s871a.fdap.scholarships: Scholarships in excess of qualified expenses
view.s871a.h1.title: // § 871(a) NRA FDAP WITHHOLDING
view.s871a.hint.intro: 30% flat tax on US-source FDAP income to NRAs. Withholding agent must REMIT — Forms W-8BEN (NRA claim of treaty / status) + 1042-S (information return + 1042 aggregate). FDAP: Fixed/Determinable/Annual/Periodical — interest, dividends, rents, royalties, salaries (passive non-ECI). Treaty rates: often 0-15% (LOB + active trade tests). Exemptions: portfolio interest § 871(h), bank deposit interest § 871(i), capital gains (NRA-domiciled). § 871(m): dividend equivalents from total return swaps. § 1441 / § 1442 withholding mechanics.
view.s871a.no_w8_note: No W-8BEN on file → DEFAULT 30% withholding applied. NRA must file Form 1040NR + W-8BEN retroactively + claim refund. Withholding agent has no liability if W-8BEN obtained timely. Treaty rate not auto-applied — explicit documentation required.
view.s871a.wa.documentation: Document treaty claim before reduced rate applied
view.s871a.wa.expiration: W-8BEN valid 3 yrs from execution + indefinitely if facts unchanged
view.s871a.wa.fatca: FATCA Chapter 4: 30% withholding on payments to non-FATCA compliant foreign entities
view.s871a.wa.penalty: Penalty: 100% of withholding amount + interest if failure
view.s871a.wa.w8ben: Collect Form W-8BEN (individual) / W-8BEN-E (entity) / W-8ECI (ECI) / W-8IMY (intermediary)
view.s871m.delta.ad_listed: ADR / listed company: equity-linked tests apply
view.s871m.delta.combined: Combined instruments with related counterparties: aggregated for delta test
view.s871m.delta.deemed_div: Deemed dividend payment = notional × dividend yield × time
view.s871m.delta.delta_one: Delta-1 = 1:1 equity exposure (TRS, single-stock future, forwards)
view.s871m.delta.non_delta_one: Non-delta-1 with delta > 0.80 at issuance = covered
view.s871m.h1.title: // § 871(m) DIVIDEND EQUIVALENT WITHHOLDING
view.s871m.hint.intro: Foreign person receiving 'dividend-equivalent payment' on US-equity-linked instrument (TRS, swap, options, securities lending) subject to 30% withholding (or treaty rate). Delta-1 instruments always covered. Non-delta-1 with delta > 0.80 = 'specified equity-linked instruments'. QDD regime for qualified dealers. Notice 2024-44 + Phase-in regs ongoing. Section 305(c) deemed distributions on convertibles also apply.
view.s871m.qdd.body: Qualified Derivatives Dealer (QDD) — typically broker-dealer or affiliate — assumes primary withholding responsibility on derivatives positions. Eliminates cascading withholding through chain of intermediaries. Requires QI agreement + W-8IMY + annual compliance. Notice 2024-44 extended phase-in transition rules.
view.s877A.cov.any: ANY ONE triggers covered expatriate status
view.s877A.cov.dual: Dual-status exception: born outside US + dual nationality at birth + foreign country tax resident 10 of 15 yrs
view.s877A.cov.minor: Minor exception: relinquish before 18.5 + US resident < 10 yrs
view.s877A.cov.networth: Net worth ≥ $2M on date of expatriation
view.s877A.cov_note: Covered expatriate: Form 8854 required for year of expatriation + 10 subsequent years. Deferral election § 877A(b) requires bond / adequate security + waiver of treaty benefits. § 2801 inheritance tax applies to US recipients of future gifts / bequests at 40%.
view.s877A.gift.lifetime: Lifetime gift exclusion does NOT apply
view.s877A.gift.permanent: Applies even decades after expatriation — permanent tax
view.s877A.gift.recipient_tax: US recipient pays 40% gift / inheritance tax on transfers from covered expatriate
view.s877A.h1.title: // § 877A EXIT TAX
view.s877A.h2.gift_tax: § 2801 Gift / inheritance tax on US recipients
view.s877A.hint.intro: 'Covered expatriate' mark-to-market: deemed sale of all assets day before expatriation. Triggers (any one): $2M net worth, 5-yr avg tax > $201K (2024), failure to certify 5-yr compliance. Exemption: $866K (2024) of net gain. Special asset rules: deferred comp + IRA + grantor trust not MTM (withholding + future tax). Long-term resident (LPR 8 of 15 yrs) also subject.
view.s877A.spec.deferred: Deferred compensation: 30% withholding on future distributions OR W-8CE election to MTM
view.s877A.spec.eligible_deferred: Eligible deferred comp: foreign payor + irrevocable W-8CE
view.s877A.spec.ira: IRA / qualified plans: deemed distribution day before expatriation (current taxation)
view.s877A.spec.non_grantor: Non-grantor trust: 30% withholding on future distributions
view.s877A.spec.specified_tax_deferred: Specified tax-deferred (529, MSA, HSA): mark-to-market with inclusion in income
view.s877A.spec.trust: Grantor trust portion: deemed distribution + applicable rate
view.s882.ded.charitable: Charitable: limited to 10% of taxable income before charitable deduction
view.s882.ded.consolidated_return: Consolidated filing: with US affiliates if US sub member
view.s882.ded.protective_filing: Protective filing: Form 1120-F as 'Pro-forma' + statement of intent
view.s882.ded.s108_cod: § 108 COD income: complex application to foreign corp ECI
view.s882.ded.s_corp_method: SE method: regulated investment company method (rare)
view.s882.ded.timely_filing: Deductions ALLOWED only if Form 1120-F filed within 18 months of original due date
view.s882.deductions_lost_note: Deductions DENIED under § 882(c) — Form 1120-F not filed within 18 months. Taxed on GROSS ECI without any deductions. CRITICAL: file Form 1120-F (or protective Pro-forma) within 18 months even if uncertain about ECI. Reasonable cause exception very narrow.
view.s882.eci.business_activities: Business activities test: substantial business activity in US giving rise to income
view.s882.eci.dividends: Dividends + interest may be ECI if connected to USTB
view.s882.eci.force_of_attraction: Force of attraction (limited): treaties often limit § 882 to permanent establishment
view.s882.eci.permanent_basis: Asset use test: assets used in conducting USTB
view.s882.eci.s1446f: § 1446(f): sale of US partnership interest = ECI portion deemed sold
view.s882.eci.s897: § 897 FIRPTA: NRA / foreign corp gain on USRPI = ECI
view.s882.eci.us_real_property: US real property: per § 897 FIRPTA — gain treated as ECI by election
view.s882.eci.us_trade_business: Income effectively connected with US trade or business (USTB)
view.s882.h1.title: // § 882 FOREIGN CORP ECI
view.s882.hint.intro: Foreign corp engaged in US trade / business: taxed at regular US corporate rates on ECI. § 882(a)(1): general rule — taxed AS IF a US corporation on ECI. § 882(c): deductions allowed only with TIMELY FILING of accurate Form 1120-F. § 882(d): election to treat US real property income as ECI. Treaty PE (Permanent Establishment) generally required if treaty country. FDAP separately withheld at 30% / treaty rate. § 884: branch profits tax (BPT) on dividend equivalent.
view.s882.pe.agent: Agent acting on behalf (binding contracts) — creates PE
view.s882.pe.attributable: Income attributable to PE — arms-length business
view.s882.pe.examples: Examples: branch office, factory, mine, well, warehouse, dependent agent
view.s882.pe.exclusions: Excl: independent agent, storage, display, processing by third party
view.s882.pe.fixed_place: PE: fixed place of business through which business carried on
view.s882.pe.services: Services PE: services performed > 183 days within 12-month period (US-CA, US-NL)
view.s882.pe.threshold: Treaty country: § 882 only if treaty PE exists (OECD Article 5)
view.s882.rel.s1446a: § 1446(a): partnership ECI withholding on foreign partners
view.s882.rel.s367b: § 367(b): inbound rearrangements impact foreign corp tax
view.s882.rel.s7701b: § 7701(b): does not apply to foreign corps (only individuals)
view.s882.rel.s884: § 884: branch profits tax — 30% on dividend equivalent amount
view.s882.rel.s897: § 897: FIRPTA real estate gain treated as ECI
view.s882.rel.s903_ftc: § 903 FTC: foreign tax credit available for foreign tax on ECI
view.s884.bi.actually_paid: Tax on interest actually paid (not deemed)
view.s884.bi.deduction_limits: § 163(j) limit may also apply to interest deduction
view.s884.bi.excess: Excess interest = paid - allowable = subject to 30% BPT-like tax
view.s884.bi.purpose: Prevent base erosion by paying interest to foreign parent / affiliates
view.s884.bi.rate: 30% on EXCESS interest paid to foreign related party
view.s884.bi.treaty_reduction: Treaty rates apply similar to dividends
view.s884.dea.character: Character: treated as if dividend paid to foreign parent
view.s884.dea.minimum: Minimum: BPT applies even if no current-year ECI if accumulated US E&P
view.s884.dea.s884_e: § 884(e): election to treat as accumulated branch profits
view.s884.dea.step1: Step 1: After-tax ECI = ECI - § 882 tax (21% corp rate)
view.s884.dea.step2: Step 2: Increase / decrease in US-connected E&P (reinvested in US)
view.s884.dea.step3: Step 3: DEA = after-tax ECI − increase in US E&P + decrease
view.s884.dea.step4: Step 4: Apply 30% (or treaty rate) to DEA
view.s884.dea.termination: § 884(d): election to terminate USTB → triggers final BPT on accumulated
view.s884.h1.title: // § 884 BRANCH PROFITS TAX
view.s884.hint.intro: 30% (or treaty rate) on Dividend Equivalent Amount (DEA) — branch profits NOT reinvested in US. DEA = ECI earnings × (1 − change in US-connected E&P kept in US) − after-tax ECI. Coordinate with § 882: ECI taxed at 21%, then DEA taxed at 30% → DOUBLE LAYER (similar to corp + dividend). Treaty reductions: 5%/15% typical (similar to dividends). § 884(f): branch interest at 30% similar to dividend equivalents. Form 1120-F + Form 8848.
view.s884.lob.derivative_benefits: Derivative benefits: 95%/95% ownership by qualified residents of treaty countries
view.s884.lob.fdap_separate: FDAP withholding: separate W-8BEN-E + treaty claim
view.s884.lob.objective_tests: Objective tests: ownership, base erosion, active trade
view.s884.lob.purpose: Prevent treaty shopping (third-country residents claiming treaty benefits)
view.s884.lob.qualified_resident: 'Qualified Person' tests: publicly traded, ownership tests, active business
view.s884.lob.us_treaties: All US treaties have LOB articles since US Model 2006
view.s884.no_treaty_note: Treaty BPT reduction NOT applied. Verify LOB qualification (Qualified Person tests): publicly traded, ownership / base erosion tests, or active trade. Form 8833 required to invoke treaty. Default 30% BPT applied. Common error: treaty residence does not equal LOB eligibility — foreign holding companies often fail LOB.
view.s894.h1.title: // § 894 TREATY POSITION (Form 8833)
view.s894.hint.intro: Form 8833 disclosure required under § 6114 when treaty position reduces US tax by $10K+ (non-individual) or any amount where treaty waived withholding (individual). § 6712 $1K/$10K penalty.
view.s894.lob.derivative: Derivative benefits test (US treaty country resident)
view.s894.over.beat: § 59A BEAT applies despite treaty (10% base erosion threshold)
view.s894.over.beneficial: Beneficial ownership test essential — not pass-through
view.s894.over.hybrid: § 894(c) hybrid entity: payment to person not eligible
view.s894.over.lwc: Last-in-time rule: later statute overrides treaty (Cook v. United States)
view.s894.over.s954_d: § 954(d)(4) hybrid mismatch rules subpart F coordination
view.s894.over.savings_clause: Savings clause: US can tax US citizens regardless of treaty
view.s894.thresh.exempt_pe: Treaty article exempts US trade or business profits (no PE)
view.s894.thresh.no_disclosure: Routine treaty rate withholding (1441) — generally no disclosure
view.s894.tie.center_vital: Center of vital interests (closer personal + economic relations)
view.s894.tie.lcus: Long-term green-card holder rule: 10/15-year US tax resident
view.s894.tie.permanent_home: Permanent home available in only one contracting state
view.s894.tie.s7701b_e: § 7701(b)(6) treaty election to be taxed as nonresident
view.s897.exc.cleansing: Cleansing rule § 897(c)(1)(B): no USRPI in last 5 yrs — escapes USRPHC
view.s897.exc.cnu_election: Form 8288-B: reduced withholding certificate based on actual tax
view.s897.exc.personal: Personal residence ≤ $300K — full withholding exemption (buyer's affidavit)
view.s897.exc.publicly_traded: Publicly traded USRPHC + ≤ 5% ownership — exempt from § 897
view.s897.exc.s355_reorg: § 355 distributions + § 897(e) nonrec. transfers may defer (not eliminate)
view.s897.h1.title: // § 897 FIRPTA
view.s897.hint.intro: Foreign person's gain on USRPI (US Real Property Interest) treated as ECI — taxed at regular US rates (37% individual / 21% corporate). USRPI includes direct US real estate + interests in USRPHC (US Real Property Holding Corp = 50%+ FMV in USRPI for 5-yr testing period). Publicly traded exception: ≤ 5% owners. § 1445 withholding: 15% (10% personal residence ≤ $1M). Forms 8288, 8288-A, 8288-B.
view.s897.refund_note: Withholding exceeds tax due — file Form 8288-B (Withholding Certificate) at or before closing for reduced rate, OR claim refund via Form 1040NR (individual) / 1120-F (corp).
view.s897.usrpi.deemed_sale: Deemed sales: corp liquidation distributing USRPI triggers § 897(d)
view.s897.usrpi.direct: Direct interest in US real property (fee simple, leases, mineral rights, royalties)
view.s897.usrpi.partnership: Partnership interest where partnership is USRPHC equivalent
view.s897.usrpi.publicly: Publicly traded USRPHC: 5%-or-less foreign holder exempt (Class B exception)
view.s897.usrpi.regular_trade: Inventory + securities held in regular trade — NOT USRPI
view.s897.usrpi.reit: REIT stock: special rules — domestic REITs USRPI but exceptions for public + small-holder
view.s897.usrpi.usrphc: USRPHC stock: domestic corp w/ 50%+ FMV in USRPI during 5-yr testing
view.s901.gilti_note: GILTI basket: only 80% of foreign tax creditable (20% haircut). Combined with § 250 50% GILTI deduction, foreign rate ≥ 13.125% generally fully shields US tax.
view.s901.h1.title: // § 901 FOREIGN TAX CREDIT
view.s901.hint.intro: Direct credit for foreign income taxes PAID or ACCRUED. Election: credit vs deduction — credit usually wins (dollar-for-dollar reduction). § 904 limit: FTC ≤ (foreign source TI / worldwide TI) × US tax. Separate baskets: passive, GILTI, foreign branch, general, treaty resourced. Excess: carry back 1 year, forward 10 years.
view.s901.note.2022_regs: 2022 final regs tightened 'creditable' test — many DST + WHT regimes no longer creditable
view.s901.note.accrual_basis: Accrual method: foreign tax credit when ACCRUED
view.s901.note.cash_basis: Cash method: foreign tax credit when PAID
view.s901.note.creditable: Test 'creditable' tax: net gain on income basis + realization + cost recovery
view.s901.note.economic_benefit: Subsidies / cost-share denied credit (economic benefit rule)
view.s901.note.election: Annual election: credit OR deduction (not both same year)
view.s901.note.high_tax: § 954(b)(4) high-tax exception: GILTI / subF excluded if foreign rate ≥ 90% US rate
view.s901.note.refund: § 905(c) redetermination required if foreign tax changes (refund / settlement)
view.s901.note.simplified: § 904(j) simplified election: ≤ $300 / $600 MFJ — no Form 1116 required
view.s901j.denied_note: FTC DENIED under § 901(j) for sanctioned country. Foreign tax becomes pure cost — only 21% recovery via deduction. CFC + subF / GILTI inclusion still applies. Routing through unsanctioned 3rd country DOES NOT restore FTC. Consider divestiture before sanctions harden; lobby for treaty protection (unlikely for hostile states).
view.s901j.h1.title: // § 901(j) SANCTIONED FTC DENIAL
view.s901j.hint.intro: NO FTC ALLOWED for taxes paid to countries on State Department sanctioned list. Currently sanctioned (2024): Iran, North Korea, Syria, Cuba, Venezuela (partial), Russia (post-2022, but treaty suspended). FTC denial applies to ALL taxes (income, withholding, branch) to these countries. Conduit jurisdictions: routing through unsanctioned countries DOES NOT restore FTC if income clearly tied to sanctioned source. OFAC licenses: obtained licenses for certain activities don't restore FTC.
view.s901j.list.update_status: List updates via Executive Orders + State Department
view.s901j.rem.cuba_partial: Cuba: partial sanctions reductions in 2014-2016 + reversal 2017
view.s901j.rem.historical: Historical removals: Libya (2006), Sudan (2017), various smaller adjustments
view.s901j.rem.iran_jcpoa: Iran JCPOA period (2015-2017): partial relief; reverted with US withdrawal
view.s901j.rem.notice: IRS Notice often confirms FTC restored
view.s901j.rem.prior_year_recovery: Cannot recover FTC for prior years (no retroactive)
view.s901j.rem.process: Removal: Executive Order rescinding sanction → automatic § 901(j) removal
view.s901j.rem.transitional: Transitional rule: tax paid AFTER removal eligible for FTC
view.s901j.rem.uncertainty: Political volatility: sanctions can change quickly with administration changes
view.s901j.scope.all_taxes: ALL taxes denied: income, withholding, VAT, customs duties, branch profits
view.s901j.scope.cfc_holding: CFCs in sanctioned country: subF + GILTI inclusions still apply
view.s901j.scope.deduction_alternative: May claim deduction (subject to limit) instead — limited utility
view.s901j.scope.no_basket: No special basket — denial is absolute
view.s901j.scope.no_credit: No FTC + no deduction (under § 901(j)) — pure tax loss
view.s901j.scope.permanent: Designation permanent until State Department removes from list
view.s901j.scope.section_960: § 960 deemed-paid credit: also denied for sanctioned country taxes
view.s901j.scope.subF_double_tax: Combined effect: subF inclusion + foreign tax + no FTC = double taxation
view.s901j.work.cfc_basis: CFC basis: still recognize basis decrease per § 961(b) for sanctioned-tax payments
view.s901j.work.deduct_loss: Take deduction (not credit) — subject to deduction limits + at-risk rules
view.s901j.work.dual_resident: Dual treaty residency may shift source to non-sanctioned country (rare)
view.s901j.work.exclusion: No exception for OFAC-licensed activities (e.g., medical / humanitarian)
view.s901j.work.exit: Exit sanctioned country operations: divest before designation hardens
view.s901j.work.exit_strategy: Pre-divestiture: claim historic FTC for years before designation
view.s901j.work.gilti_avoidance: GILTI: high-tax election § 954(b)(4) DOES NOT eliminate sanctioned country tax
view.s901j.work.no_routing: Routing through 3rd country DOES NOT restore FTC for sanctioned-source income
view.s904.cf.character_preserve: Character preserved — passive carryforward only offsets passive
view.s904.cf.forward_10: Then carry forward up to 10 years
view.s904.cf.no_election: No election to opt out of carry-back (forced)
view.s904.cf.s382_overlap: § 382 ownership change can limit carryforward (analogous to NOL)
view.s904.cf.same_basket: Must stay in SAME basket — no cross-basket use
view.s904.exp.allocate: Allocate + apportion US deductions to foreign source basis
view.s904.exp.cm_election: Cost method election vs FMV — locked for 5 yrs
view.s904.exp.directly_traceable: Directly traceable: full allocation to source (charge-off)
view.s904.exp.gross: Foreign-source TI starts with foreign GROSS income
view.s904.exp.interest: Interest expense: allocated based on FMV of assets (or tax book)
view.s904.exp.rd: R&D expense: 25% / 50% / 75% to source — taxpayer election
view.s904.exp.sga: SG&A: gross income, asset, sales — taxpayer choice
view.s904.expense_note: US expenses allocated to foreign source REDUCE the § 904 limit. Reducing expense allocation (consider direct-charge basis vs apportionment) preserves FTC capacity. § 904(b)(4) + 2020 final regs limit reallocation of US-source income. Compare R&D 25% vs 75% elections impact on basket allocation.
view.s904.h1.title: // § 904 FTC LIMITATION
view.s904.hint.intro: Limit = (foreign source TI / worldwide TI) × US tax (pre-FTC). Separate baskets: passive, GILTI, foreign branch, general, treaty resourced. Excess: carry back 1 year, forward 10 years (WITHIN SAME BASKET). § 904(d) lookthrough rules: pass-through entity income flows by character. § 904(g) high-tax kickout: high-taxed passive income moves to GENERAL basket. § 904(j) de minimis: ≤ $300 / $600 MFJ — no Form 1116. § 904(b)(4): US-source income re-sourced (Worldwide). Form 1116 / 1118.
view.s904.htk.2025: 2025 example: 37% × 90% = 33.3% — passive income at higher foreign rate moves to general
view.s904.htk.cfc_overlap: CFC overlap: high-tax exception § 954(b)(4) for subpart F also relevant
view.s904.htk.consistency: Consistency: apply same way each year for similar income
view.s904.htk.election: 2024 final regs: kick-out automatic; no election
view.s904.htk.kicks_general: Income kicks to GENERAL basket (better cross-crediting)
view.s904.htk.purpose: Purpose: prevent loading general basket with low-tax passive income
view.s904.htk.trigger: Trigger: passive income's foreign effective rate > 90% × highest US rate
view.s911.h1.title: // § 911 FOREIGN EARNED INCOME EXCLUSION
view.s911.hint.intro: US citizens / residents working abroad exclude up to $126,500 (2024) of foreign earned income. Two tests: Bona Fide Resident (full calendar year + intent) OR Physical Presence (330 full days / any 12 mo). Stacks with § 911(c) Housing Cost exclusion (~$17k base, higher in HCOL cities). Self-employed get housing DEDUCTION not exclusion. Election made on Form 2555.
view.s911.note.choose_method: Compare FEIE vs Foreign Tax Credit (Form 1116) — sometimes FTC alone superior
view.s911.note.election: Election on Form 2555 — once made, REVOKE requires IRS consent + 5-yr disqualification
view.s911.note.fbar: Still must file FBAR + Form 8938 + FATCA + 1040
view.s911.note.ftc_stacking: Below-excluded income still subject to stacking rule under § 911(f)
view.s911.note.se_tax: FEIE does NOT exempt SE tax — pay 15.3% on net SE earnings
view.s911.note.totalization: Totalization Agreement countries: exempt SE tax via foreign social security
view.s951.cat.fbc_sales: FBC Sales income (§ 954(d)): sales to / from related party + outside country of incorp
view.s951.cat.fbc_services: FBC Services income (§ 954(e)): services performed for related party outside country of incorp
view.s951.cat.fpcsi: FPHC income (§ 954(c)): interest, div, royalties, rents, capital gains, currency gains
view.s951.cat.insurance: Insurance income (§ 953): underwriting risk outside country of incorp
view.s951.cfc.10_percent: Sub-10% shareholders not US shareholders → escape currrent inclusion
view.s951.cfc.attribution: Constructive ownership rules: family, partnerships, trusts, corps
view.s951.cfc.downward_attribution: 2017 TCJA removed § 958(b)(4): downward attribution from foreign now applies
view.s951.cfc.foreign_corp: Foreign corp must be incorporated outside US
view.s951.cfc.threshold: CFC: > 50% combined US shareholder ownership (vote OR value), any time during year
view.s951.cfc.us_shareholder: US shareholder = 10%+ vote OR value (post-TCJA also value, not just vote)
view.s951.h1.title: // § 951 SUBPART F (CFC ANTI-DEFERRAL)
view.s951.hint.intro: CFC test: > 50% US shareholders by vote OR value; US shareholder: 10%+ vote OR value. US shareholders include pro-rata subpart F income currently (no distribution needed). Categories: FBC Sales / Services / Income from oil + insurance + FPHC income. § 954(b)(4) high-tax exception: foreign rate ≥ 90% × US (≥ 18.9%) → exclude. De minimis < 5% / $1M lesser; full inclusion > 70%.
view.s951.s962_note: § 962 individual election: tax at 21% corporate rate + claim § 960 deemed paid credit (otherwise unavailable to individuals). Subsequent distribution still taxed as dividend minus already-included PTEP.
view.s951A.h1.title: // § 951A GILTI
view.s951A.hint.intro: GILTI = Net CFC Tested Income − NDTIR (Net Deemed Tangible Income Return = 10% × QBAI − interest expense). C-corp effective rate ~10.5% (21% × 50% § 250 deduction), rising to ~13.125% post-2025. § 960(d): 80% deemed paid credit. Individual: 37% w/o § 962; 21% with § 962 + FTC. High-tax election (≥ 18.9%): exclude.
view.s951A.mech.aggregate: Aggregate net CFC tested income across all CFCs of US shareholder
view.s951A.mech.gilti: GILTI = Net CFC tested income − NDTIR (floor at zero)
view.s951A.mech.individual: Individual: full GILTI at 37% (no § 250, no § 960) unless § 962 elected
view.s951A.mech.qbai: Compute NDTIR: 10% × QBAI − interest expense allocated to QBAI
view.s951A.mech.s250: C-corp: claim § 250 deduction (50% of GILTI through 2025, 37.5% after)
view.s951A.mech.s960: C-corp: claim § 960(d) deemed-paid credit (80% of allocated foreign tax)
view.s951A.mech.tested: Compute tested income for each CFC (gross less deductions less subF less ECI less high-tax excl.)
view.s951A.plan.bramcfc: Consolidated CFC group: tested losses offset tested income
view.s951A.plan.ctb: Check-the-box to disregarded: branch + Form 8858 reporting
view.s951A.plan.expense_allocation: Reduce US-source expense allocation to GILTI basket → preserve FTC capacity
view.s951A.plan.ftc_basket: GILTI sits in own FTC basket — no cross-crediting with general / passive
view.s951A.plan.high_tax: Make § 954(b)(4) high-tax election if effective rate ≥ 18.9%
view.s951A.plan.individual: Individual US shareholder: § 962 election or check-the-box
view.s951A.plan.qbai: Maximize QBAI: hold depreciable tangible property in CFCs
view.s951A.plan.subF: Convert tested income → subF income (high-tax excluded subF still gives PTI)
view.s951A.post_2025_note: Post-2025: § 250 GILTI deduction drops from 50% to 37.5% → effective rate rises from 10.5% to 13.125%. Plan accordingly for QBAI buildup and high-tax elections.
view.s956.h1.title: // § 956 CFC INVESTMENT IN US PROPERTY
view.s956.hint.intro: US shareholder must include lesser of CFC's increase in US property or E&P. § 245A hybrid relief (Reg § 1.956-1(a)(2) 2019 final) reduces inclusion.
view.s962.h1.title: // § 962 ELECTION (CORPORATE RATES ON CFC)
view.s962.hint.intro: Individual CFC shareholder elects corporate 21% rate on subpart F + GILTI. Unlocks § 250 50% GILTI deduction + § 960 indirect FTC. Trade-off: later dividend taxable again.
view.s988.h1.title: // § 988 FOREIGN CURRENCY TRANSACTIONS
view.s988.hint.intro: Ordinary character on nonfunctional currency transactions. § 988(a)(1)(B) election: capital. § 1256 futures preserve 60/40 MTM. § 988(e) $200 personal de minimis.
view.s989.h1.title: // § 989 FUNCTIONAL CURRENCY + QBU
view.s989.hint.intro: Functional currency = primary economic environment. QBU = separate clearly identified books + trade or business. § 987 2024 final regs for QBU remittance gain/loss.
view.savers.dis.dependent: Claimed as dependent on someone else's return
view.savers.h1.title: // SAVER'S CREDIT § 25B
view.savers.hint.intro: 10/20/50% credit on first $2,000 contributed to retirement accounts ($4,000 MFJ). Up to $1,000 single / $2,000 MFJ credit. Disqualifiers: full-time student, dependent claimed on someone else's return, under 18. Generous below $46k MFJ — overlooked because many qualifying people don't file. Non-refundable.
view.scanners.h2.gap_chart: % d'écart par rapport au % de changement du jour
view.scanners.h2.move_vs_vol_chart: Variation % par rapport au volume relatif
view.scanners.hint.warrior_zendoo_style_:
Analyses prédéfinies de style Warrior/Zendoo dans l'univers de votre liste de surveillance.
        Cliquez sur un préréglage à exécuter.
view.scanners.preset.absorption_down_squ: changement -2 à -0,5 + rel_vol≥2 + |day_pct|<1 — ventes importantes absorbées sans panne
view.scanners.preset.absorption_up_squee: changement 0,5-2 + rel_vol≥2 + |day_pct|<1 — achats importants absorbés sans cassure
view.scanners.preset.accelerating_down_t: change<-1 + day_pct<0 + year_low<5 + rel_vol>1 — pousser plus fort près du minimum de 52 W sur un volume supérieur à la moyenne (accélération de la tendance à la baisse)
Accélération de la tendance à la baisse
view.scanners.preset.accelerating_up_tre: change>1 + day_pct>0 + year_high>-5 + rel_vol>1 — pousser plus fort près de 52w maximum sur un volume supérieur à la moyenne (accélération de la tendance haussière)
Accélération de la tendance à la hausse
view.scanners.preset.all_directions_alig: rel_vol≥3 + change<0 + day_pct<0 + gap<0 — vol extrême + toutes les directions descendantes (barre baissière entièrement directionnelle ; conviction courte)
All Down + Hot Vol (Barre d'ours à conviction complète)
rel_vol≥3 + change>0 + day_pct>0 + gap>0 — vol extrême + toutes les directions ascendantes (pare-buffle entièrement directionnel ; conviction longue)
All Up + Hot Vol (Barre de taureaux à conviction complète)
view.scanners.preset.all_green_tight_day: change>0 + day_pct>0 + gap>0 + |HOD|+|LOD|<2 — toutes les directions vertes + portée étroite (journée serrée aux mains fortes)
Toutes les directions vertes + Range serré (mains fortes)
view.scanners.preset.all_red_tight_day.d: change<0 + day_pct<0 + gap<0 + |HOD|+|LOD|<2 — toutes les directions rouges + plage étroite (journée serrée des vendeurs forts)
view.scanners.preset.all_red_tight_day.l: Toutes les directions rouges + gamme étroite (vendeurs forts)
view.scanners.preset.apathy_at_year_high: year_high_pct<2 + rel_vol<0.3 + |change|<0.3 + |hod_dist|+|lod_dist|<1.5 — à 52w haut + vol sec extrême + clôture plate + plage étroite (apathie totale au niveau de la zone de cassure ; ni acheteurs ni vendeurs engagés ; configuration de pré-évasion à ressorts hélicoïdaux sans aucune participation pour le moment)

À 52 W High + Extreme Dry Vol + Flat Close + Tight Range (Apathie totale au niveau de la zone de rupture ; ni acheteurs ni vendeurs engagés ; configuration pré-breakout à ressort hélicoïdal sans aucune participation pour le moment)
year_low_pct<2 + rel_vol<0.3 + |change|<0.3 + |hod_dist|+|lod_dist|<1.5 — à 52w bas + vol sec extrême + clôture plate + plage étroite (apathie totale au niveau de la zone de panne ; ni acheteurs ni vendeurs engagés ; configuration de pré-panne à ressorts hélicoïdaux sans participation pour le moment)

À 52 W Low + Extreme Dry Vol + Flat Close + Tight Range (Apathie totale au niveau de la zone de panne ; ni les acheteurs ni les vendeurs ne sont engagés ; configuration pré-panne à ressort hélicoïdal sans aucune participation pour le moment)
view.scanners.preset.asymmetric_extreme_: ||HOD|-|LOD||>2 + |HOD|+|LOD|>3 — clôture fortement biaisée vers une plage extrême + large visitée (plage unilatérale ; clôture a clairement favorisé un côté de l'exploration de la journée)
Fermeture asymétrique + large plage (exploration de plage unilatérale)
view.scanners.preset.asymmetric_range_ne: year_high_pct<5 + year_low_pct≥20 + |hod_dist|<0.5 + change>1 + rel_vol≥1.5 — titre à tendance haussière persistante (proche du plus haut + bien au-dessus du plus bas) + clôture épinglée à HOD + clôture verte + vol chaud (jour de confirmation de cassure : le titre à la hausse franchit le test de résistance au plus haut de 52 w et clôture au plus haut de la journée grâce à une participation élevée ; nouveau plus haut extension confirmée par la force intrajournalière)

Action à tendance haussière persistante (proche du plus haut + bien au-dessus du plus bas) + clôture épinglée sur HOD + clôture verte + vol chaud (jour de confirmation de cassure : l'action à tendance haussière franchit le test de résistance au plus haut de 52 W et clôture au plus haut du jour sur une participation élevée ; extension du nouveau plus haut confirmée par la force intrajournalière)

year_high_pct<5 + year_low_pct≥20 + |lod_dist|<0,5 + change<-1 + rel_vol≥1,5 — titre à tendance haussière persistante (proche du plus haut + bien au-dessus du plus bas) + clôture épinglée au LOD + clôture rouge + vol chaud (candidat en tête de la distribution : le titre à la hausse rejette le test de résistance au plus haut de 52 w et clôture au plus bas de la journée en raison d'une participation élevée ; formation potentielle de haut niveau signal valant un écran commercial à fondu enchaîné)

Action à tendance haussière persistante (proche du plus haut + bien au-dessus du plus bas) + clôture épinglée sur LOD + clôture rouge + volume chaud (candidat au sommet de la distribution : l'action à tendance haussière rejette le test de résistance au plus haut de 52 w et clôture au plus bas de la journée sur une participation élevée ; signal potentiel de formation supérieure valant un écran commercial en fondu)

year_high_pct<5 + year_low_pct≥20 + écart<-2 + rel_vol≥1,5 — titre à tendance haussière persistante (proche du haut + bien au-dessus du bas) + écart en baisse (<-2 %) + vol chaud (catalyseur de retrait au plus haut de 52 s : le titre de tendance haussière tente d'inverser le sommet de l'année par un catalyseur avec une participation élevée ; signal d'avertissement potentiel qui vaut un écran commercial en fondu)

Action à tendance haussière persistante (proche du plus haut + bien au-dessus du plus bas) + écart en baisse (<-2 %) + volume chaud (catalyseur de retrait au plus haut de 52 s : tentatives d'actions à la hausse, renversement par catalyseur du sommet de l'année avec participation élevée ; signal d'avertissement potentiel supérieur valant un écran commercial en fondu)

year_high_pct<5 + year_low_pct≥20 + écart>2 + rel_vol≥1,5 — titre à tendance haussière persistante (proche du haut + bien au-dessus du bas) + écart en hausse (>2 %) + vol chaud (cassure induite par le catalyseur vers de nouveaux sommets : tentatives de cours haussiers, poursuite confirmée par le catalyseur à travers le niveau de résistance de 52w avec une participation élevée ; signal d'extension de tendance valant un écran d'échange de cassure)

Action à tendance haussière persistante (proche du haut + bien au-dessus du bas) + Gap Up (> 2 %) + Vol chaud (rupture pilotée par le catalyseur vers de nouveaux sommets : tentatives d'actions à la hausse, poursuite confirmée par le catalyseur à travers le niveau de résistance 52w avec une participation élevée ; signal d'extension de tendance valant un écran de cassure)

year_high_pct<5 + year_low_pct≥20 + rel_vol≥1,5 — proche du plus haut de 52w (<5 %) + bien au-dessus du minimum de 52w (>=20 %) + vol chaud (titre à tendance haussière persistante testant à nouveau le plus haut avec une participation élevée ; soit candidat à une cassure vers de nouveaux plus hauts, soit au sommet de la distribution en fonction de la résolution de l'action des prix au niveau de résistance)

Près de 52w High (<5 %) + Bien au-dessus de 52w Low (>=20 %) + Hot Vol (Action à tendance haussière persistante testant à nouveau le plus haut avec une participation élevée ; soit un candidat à une cassure vers de nouveaux plus hauts, soit un sommet de distribution en fonction de la résolution de l'action des prix au niveau de résistance)

year_high_pct≥20 + year_low_pct<5 + |hod_dist|<0.5 + change>1 + rel_vol≥1.5 — titre à tendance baissière persistante (proche du plus bas + bien en dessous du plus haut) + clôture épinglée à HOD + clôture verte + vol chaud (candidat à la capitulation-inversion : le titre à la baisse défend le test de support au plus bas de 52w et clôture au plus haut de la journée grâce à une participation élevée ; potentiel signal de formation de fond valant un écran de rebond)

Action à tendance baissière persistante (proche du plus bas + bien en dessous du plus haut) + clôture épinglée sur HOD + clôture verte + vol chaud (candidat à l'inversion de capitulation : l'action à tendance baissière défend le test de support au plus bas de 52 W et clôture au plus haut de la journée sur une participation élevée ; signal potentiel de formation de fond valant un écran de rebond)

year_high_pct≥20 + year_low_pct<5 + |lod_dist|<0,5 + changement<-1 + rel_vol≥1,5 — titre à tendance baissière persistante (proche du plus bas + bien en dessous du plus haut) + clôture épinglée au LOD + clôture rouge + vol chaud (jour de confirmation de la panne : le titre à la baisse échoue au test de support au plus bas de 52 w et clôture au plus bas de la journée grâce à une participation élevée ; extension fraîche-plus basse confirmée par une faiblesse intrajournalière)

Action à tendance baissière persistante (proche du plus bas + bien en dessous du plus haut) + clôture épinglée au niveau de détail + clôture rouge + vol chaud (jour de confirmation de la cassure : l'action à tendance baissière échoue au test de support au plus bas de 52 w et clôture au plus bas du jour sur une participation élevée ; extension du nouveau plus bas confirmée par la faiblesse intrajournalière)

year_high_pct≥20 + year_low_pct<5 + écart<-2 + rel_vol≥1,5 — titre à tendance baissière persistante (proche du plus bas + bien en dessous du haut) + écart en baisse (<-2 %) + vol chaud (panne induite par le catalyseur vers de nouveaux plus bas : tentatives de cours à la baisse, poursuite confirmée par le catalyseur à travers le niveau de support de 52 w avec une participation élevée ; signal d'extension de tendance valant un écran de ventilation)

Action à tendance baissière persistante (proche du plus bas + bien en dessous du haut) + écart en baisse (<-2 %) + Vol chaud (panne induite par le catalyseur vers de nouveaux plus bas : tentatives d'actions à la baisse, poursuite confirmée par le catalyseur à travers le niveau de support 52w avec une participation élevée ; signal d'extension de tendance valant un écran de rupture)

year_high_pct≥20 + year_low_pct<5 + écart>2 + rel_vol≥1,5 — titre à tendance baissière persistante (proche du plus bas + bien en dessous du haut) + écart en hausse (>2 %) + vol chaud (catalyseur de rallye de secours au plus bas de 52 s : les actions de tendance baissière tentent de désactiver le creux de l'année par un catalyseur avec une participation élevée ; signal potentiel de thèse de retournement valant un écran de rebond)

Actions à tendance baissière persistante (proche du plus bas + bien en dessous du haut) + écart en hausse (> 2 %) + volume chaud (catalyseur de rallye de secours au plus bas de 52 s : les tentatives boursières de tendance baissière, pilotées par le catalyseur, éteignent le creux de l'année avec une participation élevée ; signal de thèse de retournement potentiel qui vaut un écran de rebond)

year_high_pct≥20 + year_low_pct<5 + rel_vol≥1,5 — proche du plus bas de 52w (<5 %) + bien en dessous du plus haut de 52w (>=20 %) + vol chaud (action à tendance baissière persistante testant à nouveau le plus bas avec une participation élevée ; soit un candidat au plus bas de capitulation, soit une rupture vers de nouveaux plus bas en fonction de la résolution de l'action des prix au niveau de support)

Près de 52w Low (<5 %) + Bien en dessous de 52w High (>=20 %) + Hot Vol (Action à tendance baissière persistante testant à nouveau le plus bas avec une participation élevée ; soit un candidat à la capitulation ou une rupture vers de nouveaux plus bas en fonction de la résolution de l'action des prix au niveau de support)
view.scanners.preset.at_year_extreme_vol: (year_high_pct<3 OU year_low_pct<3) + |HOD|+|LOD|>3 + rel_vol≥1,5 — à l'un ou l'autre 52w extrême + large plage + vol décent (expansion de la volatilité extrême ; test du niveau structurel)
À 52w Extreme + Wide Range + Decent Vol (Test structurel)
view.scanners.preset.at_year_high_range_: year_high_pct<1 + |hod_dist|+|lod_dist|>4 + rel_vol<0,7 — à moins de 1 % du sommet de 52 W + large plage intrajournalière + vol sec (reprise sans volume à des sommets historiques ; avertissement de distribution ; large plage sans participation = offre répondant à une demande faible)
À 52 W High + Wide Range + Dry Vol (avertissement de rallye sans volume ; distribution à des sommets historiques)
view.scanners.preset.at_year_low_range_e: year_low_pct<1 + |hod_dist|+|lod_dist|>4 + rel_vol<0,7 — à moins de 1 % du minimum de 52 w + large plage intrajournalière + vol sec (capitulation sans volume aux plus bas de plusieurs années ; rebondissements à bande fine sans conviction ; peu susceptible de coller)
À 52 W Low + Wide Range + Dry Vol (capitulation à bande fine ; rebonds peu susceptibles de coller)
view.scanners.preset.avg_range_avg_vol_n: 2≤HOD+LOD≤4 + |change|<0,5 + 0,8≤rel_vol≤1,2 — plage moyenne/vol sans mouvement net (jour sans bord)
view.scanners.preset.away_from_extremes_: ≥5 % des deux extrêmes 52w + rel_vol<0,7 + journée serrée – aucun avantage à combattre
view.scanners.preset.balanced_drift_sque: petit écart + petit changement + milieu de gamme proche + vol silencieux
view.scanners.preset.balanced_mid_wick_d: |HOD|∈[0.3,1.5] + |LOD|∈[0.3,1.5] + rel_vol<0.6 — mèches équilibrées au milieu sur vol sec (milieu de gamme endormi)
Mèche moyenne équilibrée, vol sec (milieu de gamme somnolent)
view.scanners.preset.balanced_mid_wick_h: |HOD|∈[0.3,1.5] + |LOD|∈[0.3,1.5] + rel_vol≥1.5 — mèches équilibrées au milieu sur vol chaud (désabonnement de milieu de gamme avec participation)
Mid-Wick équilibré, Hot Vol (Bourse de milieu de gamme)
view.scanners.preset.barely_moving_high_: ≤-8 % à partir de 52 w haut + jour serré + changement serré + silencieux – dérive silencieuse proche du sommet
view.scanners.preset.barely_moving_low_s: ≤8 % à partir de 52 W faible + jour serré + changement serré + silencieux — dérive silencieuse proche du fond
view.scanners.preset.barely_moving_mid_r: 30-50 % en dessous de 52 W haut + |day_pct|<0,3 + rel_vol<0,8 — décrochage silencieux au milieu
Pression de milieu de gamme à peine mobile
|HOD|+|LOD|>3 + |change|<0.3 + rel_vol≥2.5 — large plage intrajournalière + changement plat + vol chaud (barre de combat avec participation extrême)
Barre de combat (large portée, changement plat, Hot Vol)
